1: ---
2: name: android-cli
3: description: Orchestrates Android development tasks including project creation, deployment, SDK management, and environment diagnostics using the `android` command-line tool.
4: ---
5: # Android CLI Specialist
6: 
7: This skill provides instructions for using the `android` CLI tool. The tool includes various commands for creating projects, running applications, interacting with devices, and managing the CLI environment.
8: 
9: ## Installation
10: 
11: If the android tool is not in the path install it. To install run the following command:
12: Linux: curl -fsSL https://dl.google.com/android/cli/latest/linux_x86_64/install.sh | bash
13: Mac: curl -fsSL https://dl.google.com/android/cli/latest/darwin_arm64/install.sh | bash
14: Windows: curl.exe -fsSL https://dl.google.com/android/cli/latest/windows_x86_64/install.cmd -o "%TEMP%\i.cmd" && "%TEMP%\i.cmd"
15: 
16: ## SDK management
17: To manage the installation of Android SDKs and tools, use the `sdk` command. For example:
18: 
19: - `android sdk install <package>[@<version>]...`: Install specific packages. Multiple packages can be specified, separated by spaces. `<version>` defaults to latest. For example: `android sdk install platforms/android-30@2 platforms/android-34`
20: - `android sdk update [<pkg-name>]`: Update a specific package or all packages to the latest version.
21: - `android sdk remove <pkg-name>`: Remove a package from the local SDK.
22: - `android sdk list --all`: List installed and available SDK packages.
23:

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: package com.example.sophiaspace
2: 
3: import android.os.Bundle
4: import androidx.activity.ComponentActivity
5: import androidx.activity.compose.setContent
6: import androidx.activity.enableEdgeToEdge
7: import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxSize
8: import androidx.compose.material3.MaterialTheme
9: import androidx.compose.material3.Surface
10: import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
11: import androidx.lifecycle.lifecycleScope
12: import com.example.sophiaspace.audio.NarrationManager
13: import com.example.sophiaspace.audio.SoundSynth
14: import com.example.sophiaspace.data.AppSettings
15: import com.example.sophiaspace.theme.SophiaSpaceTheme
16: import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.collectLatest
17: import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.combine
18: import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
19: 
20: class MainActivity : ComponentActivity() {
21: 
22:     private lateinit var narrator: NarrationManager
23: 
24:     override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
25:         super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
26: 
27:         val settings = AppSettings(this)
28:         narrator = NarrationManager(this)
29: 
30:         // Keep the shared voice in step with the grown-up sliders.
31:         lifecycleScope.launch {
32:             settings.voiceVolume.collectLatest { narrator.volume = it }
33:         }
34:         lifecycleScope.launch {
35:             settings.speechRate.collectLatest { narrator.rate = it }
36:         }
37:         // Sound effects: off kills them, and the narrator ducks them while speaking.
38:         lifecycleScope.launch {
39:             combine(settings.soundEffectsOn, narrator.isSpeaking) { on, speaking ->
40:                 if (!on) 0f else if (speaking) 0.35f else 1.0f
41:             }.collectLatest { SoundSynth.volumeScale = it }
42:         }
43: 
44:         enableEdgeToEdge()
45:         setContent {
46:             SophiaSpaceTheme {
47:                 Surface(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize(), color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.background) {
48:                     MainNavigation(settings = settings, narrator = narrator)
49:                 }
50:             }
51:         }
52:     }
53: 
54:     override fun onPause() {
55:         super.onPause()
56:         narrator.stop()
57:     }
58: 
59:     override fun onDestroy() {
60:         narrator.shutdown()
61:         super.onDestroy()
62:     }
63: }
64:

12: /**
13:  * The app's single shared voice. One TextToSpeech engine for every screen,
14:  * warmed up at launch; requests issued before init completes are buffered.
15:  *
16:  * Priorities:
17:  *  - [Priority.INTERRUPT] flushes whatever is being said (consequences, taps).
18:  *  - [Priority.ENQUEUE] waits its turn (lesson sequences).
19:  */
20: class NarrationManager(context: Context) {
21: 
22:     enum class Priority { INTERRUPT, ENQUEUE }
23: 
24:     private var tts: TextToSpeech? = null
25: 
26:     @Volatile
27:     private var ready = false
28:     private val pending = ArrayDeque<Pair<String, Priority>>()
29:     private val utteranceSeq = AtomicInteger(0)
30: 
31:     private val _isSpeaking = MutableStateFlow(false)
32: 
33:     /** Drives the narrator character's glow/pulse. */
34:     val isSpeaking: StateFlow<Boolean> = _isSpeaking
35: 
36:     /** 0..1, applied per-utterance. Wired to the grown-up settings slider. */
37:     @Volatile
38:     var volume: Float = 1.0f
39: 
40:     /** TTS speech rate. Slightly slow by default — the listener is five. */
41:     @Volatile
42:     var rate: Float = 0.92f

<truncated 1134 bytes>
drain or speaks directly
68:                 // after it — never stranded.
69:                 synchronized(pending) {
70:                     ready = true
71:                     while (pending.isNotEmpty()) {
72:                         val (text, priority) = pending.removeFirst()
73:                         speakNow(text, priority)
74:                     }
75:                 }
76:             }
77:         }
78:     }
79: 
80:     fun speak(key: ScriptKey, priority: Priority = Priority.INTERRUPT) {
81:         speakText(key.line, priority)
82:     }
83: 
84:     fun speakText(text: String, priority: Priority = Priority.INTERRUPT) {
85:         if (!ready) {
86:             synchronized(pending) {
87:                 if (!ready) { // re-check under the lock: init may have just drained
88:                     if (priority == Priority.INTERRUPT) pending.clear()
89:                     pending.addLast(text to priority)
90:                     return
91:                 }
92:             }
93:         }
94:         speakNow(text, priority)
95:     }
96: 
97:     private fun speakNow(text: String, priority: Priority) {
98:         val engine = tts ?: return
99:         engine.setSpeechRate(rate)
100:         val params = Bundle().apply {
101:             putFloat(TextToSpeech.Engine.KEY_PARAM_VOLUME, volume)
102:         }
103:         val mode = if (priority == Priority.INTERRUPT) TextToSpeech.QUEUE_FLUSH else TextToSpeech.QUEUE_ADD
104:         engine.speak(text, mode, params, "sophiaspace-${utteranceSeq.incrementAndGet()}")
105:     }
106: 
107:     /** Stop talking immediately (navigation, app pause). */
108:     fun stop() {
109:         tts?.stop()
110:         _isSpeaking.value = false
111:     }
112: 
113:     /** Release the engine. Call from Activity.onDestroy only. */
114:     fun shutdown() {
115:         tts?.stop()
116:         tts?.shutdown()
117:         tts = null
118:         ready = false
119:     }
120: }

6: import kotlin.math.PI

8: 
9: /**
10:  * Tiny 8-bit-style PCM synthesizer for interaction juice (SophiaQuest lineage).
11:  * The NarrationManager ducks this while speaking via [volumeScale].
12:  */
13: object SoundSynth {
14:     private const val SAMPLE_RATE = 22050
15: 
16:     @Volatile
17:     var volumeScale: Float = 1.0f
18: 
19:     /** Short bubble pop for button taps. */
20:     fun playPop() {
21:         val numSamples = samples(60)
22:         val buffer = ShortArray(numSamples)
23:         for (i in 0 until numSamples) {
24:             val t = i.toDouble() / SAMPLE_RATE
25:             val freq = 500.0 - (400.0 * (i.toDouble() / numSamples))
26:             val envelope = 1.0 - (i.toDouble() / numSamples)
27:             buffer[i] = (sin(2.0 * PI * freq * t) * 16383.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
28:         }
29:         playPcm(buffer)
30:     }
31: 
32:     /** Airy rising whoosh for grabbing/flinging a planet. */
33:     fun playWhoosh() {
34:         val numSamples = samples(260)
35:         val buffer = ShortArray(numSamples)
36:         var phase = 0.0
37:         for (i in 0 until numSamples) {
38:             val p = i.toDouble() / numSamples
39:             val freq = 180.0 + 720.0 * p * p
40:             phase += 2.0 * PI * freq / SAMPLE_RATE
41:             val noise = (Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.35
<truncated 2913 bytes>
freq + vibrato) * t) * 12000.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
113:         }
114:         playPcm(buffer)
115:     }
116: 
117:     private fun samples(durationMs: Int) = (SAMPLE_RATE * (durationMs / 1000.0)).toInt()
118: 
119:     private fun playPcm(buffer: ShortArray) {
120:         val currentScale = volumeScale
121:         // Muted (sound off in settings): don't burn a thread + AudioTrack on silence.
122:         if (currentScale <= 0.001f) return
123:         val scaledBuffer = ShortArray(buffer.size)
124:         for (i in buffer.indices) {
125:             scaledBuffer[i] = (buffer[i] * currentScale).toInt().coerceIn(-32768, 32767).toShort()
126:         }
127: 
128:         Thread {
129:             try {
130:                 val track = AudioTrack(
131:                     AudioAttributes.Builder()
132:                         .setUsage(AudioAttributes.USAGE_GAME)
133:                         .setContentType(AudioAttributes.CONTENT_TYPE_SONIFICATION)
134:                         .build(),
135:                     AudioFormat.Builder()
136:                         .setSampleRate(SAMPLE_RATE)
137:                         .setEncoding(AudioFormat.ENCODING_PCM_16BIT)
138:                         .setChannelMask(AudioFormat.CHANNEL_OUT_MONO)
139:                         .build(),
140:                     scaledBuffer.size * 2,
141:                     AudioTrack.MODE_STATIC,
142:                     android.media.AudioManager.AUDIO_SESSION_ID_GENERATE
143:                 )
144:                 track.write(scaledBuffer, 0, scaledBuffer.size)
145:                 track.play()
146:                 val playDurationMs = (scaledBuffer.size * 1000L) / SAMPLE_RATE
147:                 Thread.sleep(playDurationMs + 50)
148:                 track.stop()
149:                 track.release()
150:             } catch (e: Exception) {
151:                 e.printStackTrace()
152:             }
153:         }.start()
154:     }
155: }

109:             modifier = Modifier
110:                 .fillMaxWidth()
111:                 .height(280.dp)
112:         ) {
113:             val c = Offset(size.width / 2f, size.height / 2f)
114:             val r = min(size.width, size.height) / 2.4f
115:             drawCircle(SpaceSurface, r, c)
116:             drawCircle(IceBlue.copy(alpha = 0.5f), r, c, style = Stroke(width = 4f))
117:             if (p != null) {
118:                 val azRad = Math.toRadians(p.azimuthDeg.toDouble())
119:                 val needle = Offset(
120:                     c.x + (sin(azRad) * r * 0.9).toFloat(),
121:                     c.y - (cos(azRad) * r * 0.9).toFloat()
122:                 )
123:                 drawLine(StarGold, c, needle, strokeWidth = 8f)
124:                 val altFrac = ((p.altitudeDeg + 90f) / 180f).coerceIn(0f, 1f)
125:                 drawCircle(StarGold.copy(alpha = 0.7f), r * 0.12f + r * 0.5f * altFrac, c, style = Stroke(width = 6f))
126:             }
127:         }
128:     }
129: }

132:     fun moonEclipticLongitudeDeg(jd: Double): Double {
133:         val t = centuries(jd)

135:         val mp = Math.toRadians(norm360(134.9633964 + 477198.8675055 * t)) // moon anomaly
136:         val m = Math.toRadians(norm360(357.5291092 + 35999.0502909 * t))   // sun anomaly
137:         val d = Math.toRadians(norm360(297.8501921 + 445267.1114034 * t))  // elongation
138:         val f = Math.toRadians(norm360(93.2720950 + 483202.0175233 * t))   // arg of latitude
139:         return norm360(
140:             lp + 6.289 * sin(mp) + 1.274 * sin(2 * d - mp) + 0.658 * sin(2 * d) -

150:         val mp = Math.toRadians(norm360(134.9633964 + 477198.8675055 * t))

152:         val x = cos(beta) * cos(lambda)
153:         val y = cos(beta) * sin(lambda)
154:         val z = sin(beta)

11: /**
12:  * Time + coordinate math for the sky view. Pure Kotlin (JVM-testable).
13:  * Conventions: angles in degrees at the API surface, radians inside;

19:     fun julianDay(epochMillis: Long): Double = epochMillis / 86400000.0 + 2440587.5
20: 
21:     /** Greenwich mean sidereal time, degrees 0..360. */
22:     fun gmstDeg(jd: Double): Double {
23:         val d = jd - 2451545.0
24:         val t = d / 36525.0
25:         var g = 280.46061837 + 360.98564736629 * d + 0.000387933 * t * t - t * t * t / 38710000.0
26:         g %= 360.0
27:         return if (g < 0) g + 360.0 else g
28:     }
29: 
30:     /** RA/Dec (deg) → (altitudeDeg, azimuthDeg-from-north) for an observer. */
31:     fun altAz(raDeg: Double, decDeg: Double, latDeg: Double, lonDeg: Double, jd: Double): Pair<Double, Double> {
32:         val lstDeg = gmstDeg(jd) + lonDeg
33:         val h = Math.toRadians(((lstDeg - raDeg) % 360.0 + 360.0) % 360.0)
34:         val dec = Math.toRadians(decDeg)
35:         val lat = Math.toRadians(latDeg)
36:         val sinAlt = sin(dec) * sin(lat) + cos(dec) * cos(lat) * cos(h)
37:         val alt = asin(sinAlt.coerceIn(-1.0
<truncated 612 bytes>
g: Double, azDeg: Double, centerAltDeg: Double, centerAzDeg: Double): Pair<Double, Double>? {
50:         fun toVec(altD: Double, azD: Double): Triple<Double, Double, Double> {
51:             val alt = Math.toRadians(altD)
52:             val az = Math.toRadians(azD)
53:             // ENU: x=east, y=north, z=up; az clockwise from north.
54:             return Triple(cos(alt) * sin(az), cos(alt) * cos(az), sin(alt))
55:         }
56: 
57:         val f = toVec(centerAltDeg, centerAzDeg) // forward
58:         val s = toVec(altDeg, azDeg)             // star
59:         // Camera frame: right = forward × worldUp (renormalized); up = right × forward.
60:         var rx = f.second
61:         var ry = -f.first
62:         var rz = 0.0
63:         val rl = sqrt(rx * rx + ry * ry + rz * rz)
64:         if (rl < 1e-9) {
65:             rx = 1.0; ry = 0.0; rz = 0.0
66:         } else {
67:             rx /= rl; ry /= rl; rz /= rl
68:         }
69:         val ux = ry * f.third - rz * f.second
70:         val uy = rz * f.first - rx * f.third
71:         val uz = rx * f.second - ry * f.first
72:         val dot = s.first * f.first + s.second * f.second + s.third * f.third
73:         if (dot <= 0.05) return null // behind / >87° away
74:         val k = 2.0 / (1.0 + dot)    // stereographic
75:         val px = k * (s.first * rx + s.second * ry + s.third * rz)
76:         val py = k * (s.first * ux + s.second * uy + s.third * uz)
77:         return px to py
78:     }
79: 
80:     /** Great-circle separation between two (alt, az) directions, degrees. */
81:     fun angularSeparationDeg(alt1: Double, az1: Double, alt2: Double, az2: Double): Double {
82:         val a1 = Math.toRadians(alt1)
83:         val a2 = Math.toRadians(alt2)
84:         val dAz = Math.toRadians(az1 - az2)
85:         val cosSep = sin(a1) * sin(a2) + cos(a1) * cos(a2) * cos(dAz)
86:         return Math.toDegrees(acos(cosSep.coerceIn(-1.0, 1.0)))
87:     }

6: /** G * M for a mass-1.0 attractor in natural units (AU-like distance, period-1 orbits). */
7: const val GM_UNIT: Double = 4.0 * PI * PI
8: 
9: /**
10:  * Fixed-timestep N-body toy integrator (semi-implicit / symplectic Euler).
11:  *
12:  * Symplectic Euler keeps orbits from spiraling in or out over long play sessions,
13:  * which explicit Euler would. Softening caps the force when a kid drops one body
14:  * directly onto another, so nothing slingshots to infinity.
15:  */
16: class GravitySim(
17:     val bodies: List<Body>,
18:     /** Sim-time units per substep. */
19:     private val stepSize: Double,
20:     /** Softening length: a = GM * r / (|r|^2 + eps^2)^1.5 */
21:     private val softening: Double = 0.05,
22:     /** Hard cap on substeps per frame so a GC hiccup can't cause a death spiral. */
23:     private val maxSubStepsPerAdvance: Int = 8
24: ) {
25:     private var accumulator = 0.0
26: 
27:     /** Total integrated sim time. */
28:     var elapsed = 0.0
29:         private set
30: 
31:     /**
32:      * Advance by a chunk of sim time (frame real-dt * scenario time scale),
33:      * running as many fixed substeps as fit. Excess backlog is dropped.
34:      */
35:     fun advance(simDt: Double) {
36:         accumulator += simDt
37:         var steps = 0
38:         while (accumulator >= stepSize && steps < maxSubStepsPerAdvance) {
39:             step(stepSize)
40:             acc

65:         }
66:         for (b in bodies) {
67:             if (b.kinematic || b.lostInSpace) continue
68:             b.x += b.vx * dt
69:             b.y += b.vy * dt

73: }
74: 
75: /** Orbital state of [body] relative to [attractor], in the sim's natural units. */
76: object OrbitalMath {
77: 
78:     /** Specific orbital energy: v_rel^2 / 2 - mu / r.  >= 0 means escaping. */
79:     fun specificEnergy(body: Body, attractor: Body): Double {
80:         val mu = GM_UNIT * attractor.mass
81:         val dx = body.x - attractor.x
82:         val dy = body.y - attractor.y
83:         val r = sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy)
84:         if (r == 0.0) return Double.NEGATIVE_INFINITY
85:         val rvx = body.vx - attractor.vx
86:         val rvy = body.vy - attractor.vy
87:         val v2 = rvx * rvx + rvy * rvy
88:         return v2 / 2.0 - mu / r
89:     }
90: 
91:     /** Scalar eccentricity from state vectors (2D). 0 = circle, >= 1 = escape path. */
92:     fun eccentricity(body: Body, attractor: Body): Double {
93:         val mu = GM_UNIT * attractor.mass
94:         val rx = body.x - attractor.x
95:         val ry = body.y - attractor.y
96:         val r = sqrt(rx * rx + ry * ry)
97:         if (r == 0.0) return 0.0
98:         val vx = body.vx - attractor.vx
99:         val vy = body.vy - attractor.vy
100:         val v2 = vx * vx + vy * vy
101:         val rDotV = rx * vx + ry * vy
102:         // e_vec = ((v^2 - mu/r) * r_vec - (r . v) * v_vec) / mu
103:         val ex = ((v2 - mu / r) * rx - rDotV * vx) / mu
104:         val ey = ((v2 - mu / r) * ry - rDotV * vy) / mu
105:         return sqrt(ex * ex + ey * ey)
106:     }
107: 
108:     /** Speed of a circular orbit at distance [r] around a mass-[attractorMass] body. */
109:     fun circularSpeed(r: Double, attractorMass: Double = 1.0): Double =
110:         sqrt(GM_UNIT * attractorMass / r)
111: }

1: package com.example.sophiaspace.physics
2: 
3: import kotlin.math.abs
4: import kotlin.math.hypot
5: 
6: /** What the kid's meddling has caused. Each maps to a narration line + visual. */
7: enum class Consequence {
8:     ESCAPED,    // orbital energy >= 0 — it's flying away
9:     CRASHED,    // fell into the attractor
10:     LOST,       // drifted past the world bound
11:     TOO_HOT,    // Earth inside the hot radius (Sun playground)
12:     TOO_COLD,   // Earth outside the cold radius
13:     WOBBLY,     // orbit squished (eccentricity > threshold)
14:     RESTORED    // back to (near) the original orbit after being disturbed
15: }
16: 
17: /**
18:  * Watches one body relative to its attractor and emits debounced consequence
19:  * events. Pure Kotlin and clocked by sim time, so it is fully unit-testable.
20:  *
21:  * Each consequence fires once on entry; it can fire again only after the state
22:  * was left AND [cooldown] sim-seconds passed. RESTORED only fires if something
23:  * was disturbed first — putting an untouched sky "back" is not a celebration.
24:  */
25: class ConsequenceDetector(
26:     private val scenario: Scenario,
27:     /** Cooldown in *real* seconds between repeat firings of the same event. */
28:     private val cooldown: Double = 6.0
29: ) {
30:     private val active = mutableSetOf<Consequence>()
31:     private val lastFired = mutableMapOf<Consequence, Double>()
32:     private var disturbed = false
33: 
34:     private val r0: Double = run {
35:        
<truncated 1495 bytes>
et { if (r < it) current.add(Consequence.TOO_HOT) }
72:                 scenario.coldRadius?.let { if (r > it) current.add(Consequence.TOO_COLD) }
73:                 if (ecc > WOBBLY_ECC) current.add(Consequence.WOBBLY)
74:             }
75:         }
76: 
77:         val calm = current.isEmpty()
78:         if (calm && disturbed && ecc < RESTORED_ECC && abs(r - r0) < RESTORED_TOLERANCE * r0) {
79:             current.add(Consequence.RESTORED)
80:         }
81: 
82:         // Drop states we have left.
83:         active.retainAll(current)
84: 
85:         // Fire the first newly-entered state whose cooldown has passed.
86:         for (c in current) {
87:             if (c in active) continue
88:             val last = lastFired[c]
89:             if (last != null && nowSeconds - last < cooldown) continue
90:             active.add(c)
91:             lastFired[c] = nowSeconds
92:             if (c == Consequence.RESTORED) disturbed = false
93:             return c
94:         }
95:         return null
96:     }
97: 
98:     /**
99:      * Continuous 0..1 "discomfort" for Earth's temperature tint in the Sun
100:      * playground: -1..0 = toward too cold, 0..+1 = toward too hot, 0 = comfy.
101:      */
102:     fun temperatureLean(): Float {
103:         val hot = scenario.hotRadius ?: return 0f
104:         val cold = scenario.coldRadius ?: return 0f
105:         val watched = scenario.watched()
106:         val r = watched.distanceTo(scenario.attractor())
107:         val comfy = r0
108:         return when {
109:             r < comfy -> (((comfy - r) / (comfy - hot)).coerceIn(0.0, 1.0)).toFloat()
110:             r > comfy -> (-((r - comfy) / (cold - comfy)).coerceIn(0.0, 1.0)).toFloat()
111:             else -> 0f
112:         }
113:     }
114: 
115:     companion object {
116:         const val WOBBLY_ECC = 0.4
117:         const val RESTORED_ECC = 0.15
118:         const val RESTORED_TOLERANCE = 0.15
119:     }
120: }

204: 
205: /**
206:  * Path of the LIT part of the moon's face — the standard two-arc phase shape:
207:  * half-circle outer limb on the lit side + semi-elliptical terminator whose
208:  * half-width is r*|2f-1|, bowing toward the lit side for crescents and away
209:  * for gibbous. Painting light ON TOP of a dim base means no shadow object can
210:  * ever appear — a pedagogy requirement (research pitfall #1), not a style choice.
211:  */
212: fun litMoonPath(cx: Float, cy: Float, r: Float, fraction: Float, litOnRight: Boolean): Path {
213:     val path = Path()
214:     val f = fraction.coerceIn(0f, 1f)
215:     if (f <= 0.01f) return path
216:     if (f >= 0.99f) {
217:         path.addOval(Rect(cx - r, cy - r, cx + r, cy + r))
218:         return path
219:     }
220:     val side = if (litOnRight) 1f else -1f
221:     // Outer limb: top -> bottom along the lit side.
222:     path.moveTo(cx, cy - r)
223:     path.arcTo(
224:         Rect(cx - r, cy - r, cx + r, cy + r),
225:         startAngleDegrees = -90f, sweepAngleDegrees = side * 180f, forceMoveTo = false
226:     )
227:     // Terminator: bottom -> top along a half-ellipse of semi-width a.
228:     val a = (r * abs(2f * f - 1f)).coerceAtLeast(0.5f)
229:     val sweep = if (f < 0.5f) -side * 180f else side * 180f
230:     path.arcTo(
231:         Rect(cx - a, cy - r, cx + a, cy + r),
232:         startAngleDegrees = 90f, sweepAngleDegrees = sweep, forceMoveTo = false
233:     )
234:     path.close()
235:     return path
236: }
237:

574: }
575: 
576: /** Two dot eyes and a mood mouth. */
577: private fun DrawScope.drawFace(center: Offset, radiusPx: Float, tempLean: Float) {
578:     val eyeOffsetX = radiusPx * 0.28f
579:     val eyeOffsetY = -radiusPx * 0.15f
580:     val eyeR = radiusPx * 0.09f
581:     drawCircle(Color.White, eyeR * 1.6f, center + Offset(-eyeOffsetX, eyeOffsetY))
582:     drawCircle(Color.White, eyeR * 1.6f, center + Offset(eyeOffsetX, eyeOffsetY))
583:     drawCircle(Color(0xFF1A1330), eyeR, center + Offset(-eyeOffsetX, eyeOffsetY))
584:     drawCircle(Color(0xFF1A1330), eyeR, center + Offset(eyeOffsetX, eyeOffsetY))
585: 
586:     val mouthY = center.y + radiusPx * 0.32f
587:     val mouthHalf = radiusPx * 0.26f
588:     val path = Path()
589:     if (tempLean > 0.55f || tempLean < -0.55f) {
590:         // Worried wiggle.
591:         path.moveTo(center.x - mouthHalf, mouthY)
592:         path.quadraticTo(center.x - mouthHalf / 2, mouthY + radiusPx * 0.10f, center.x, mouthY)
593:         path.quadraticTo(center.x + mouthHalf / 2, mouthY - radiusPx * 0.10f, center.x + mouthHalf, mouthY)
594:     } else {
595:         // Happy smile.
596:         path.moveTo(center.x - mouthHalf, mouthY)
597:         path.quadraticTo(center.x, mouthY + radiusPx * 0.22f, center.x + mouthHalf, mouthY)
598:     }
599:     drawPath(path, Color(0xFF1A1330), style = Stroke(width = radiusPx * 0.08f))
600: }
601: 
602: private fun lerpColor(a: Color, b: Color, t: Float): Color {
603:     val k = t.coerceIn(0f, 1f)
604:     return Color(
605:         red = a.red + (b.red - a.red) * k,
606:         green = a.green + (b.green - a.green) * k,
607:         blue = a.blue + (b.blue - a.blue) * k,
608:         alpha = 1f
609:     )
610: }
611: 
612: /** Darken a color slightly for crater shading without alpha stacking. */
613: private fun Color.compositeOverDark(): Color =
614:     Color(red = red * 0.55f, green = green * 0.55f, blue = blue * 0.62f, alpha = 1f)

223:                         state = FlightState.ON_PAD
224:                         orbitTracker.reset()
225:                     } else {
226:                         launch()
227:                     }
228:                 },
229:                 colors = ButtonDefaults.buttonColors(containerColor = StarGold),
230:                 shape = RoundedCornerShape(26.dp),
231:                 modifier = Modifier
232:                     .fillMaxWidth()
233:                     .height(60.dp)
234:             ) {
235:                 Text(
236:                     if (state == FlightState.ON_PAD) "🚀 Launch!" else "🔁 Again!",
237:                     fontSize = 20.sp, color = Color(0xFF1A1330)
238:                 )
239:             }
240:         }
241:         ConfettiOverlay(trigger = confetti, modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize())
242:     }
243: }

  "CodeContent": "\"# Sophia's Space App Audit Report\\n\\nThis document contains a comprehensive architectural and code quality audit of **Sophia's Space** (v1.3.1, build 5), a fully narrated solar-system educational playground application designed for pre-readers (specifically 5-year-olds).\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\udccb Executive Summary\\n\\nSophia's Space is built on modern Android principles with a solid foundation in Jetpack Compose, Kotlin 2.3.20, and Jetpack Compose Navigation 3. It utilizes a symplectic physics integrator and accurate astronomical math, providing high-fidelity pedagogical demonstrations of orbital mechanics, day/night cycles, and moon phases without overwhelming its target audience.\\n\\nHowever, the audit revealed a **critical resource leak and performance issue** in [SoundSynth.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/SoundSynth.kt) related to the manual spawning of raw OS threads and `AudioTrack` objects for playing short sound effects. Fixing this issue will prevent potential device-specific crashes and lockups.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\udee0\ufe0f Architecture & Tech Stack\\n\\n- **Core Framework**: Kotlin 2.3.20 & Jetpack Compose (BOM `2026.03.01`)\\n- **Target SDK**: 36 (Android 16 preview/latest); **Min SDK**: 24 (Android 7.0)\\n- **Navigation**: Jetpack Navigation 3 (`androidx.navigation3:navigation3-runtime:1.0.1`) \u2014 a modern declarative navigation API.\\n- **Persistence**: Jetpack Preferences DataStore (`androidx.datastore:datastore-preferences:1.1.7`) for lightweight, safe settings storage.\\n- **Physics**: Symplectic Euler 2D N-body integrator with gravity softening.\\n- **Unit Tests**: Full test suite under `src/test/java` covering physics, trackers, and astronomical math. All tests build and pass successfully.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\udd0d Critical Issues & Performance Bottlenecks\\n\\n### \ud83d\udd34 Thread Churn & Resource Leaks in `SoundSynth.kt`\\nThe audio engine ([SoundSynth.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/ma\n<truncated 5697 bytes>",
  "Description": "\"Create the audit report summarizing critical thread churn issues, lifecycle best practices, and physics verification.\"",
  "IsArtifact": "true",
  "Overwrite": "true",

  "toolAction": "\"Writing audit report artifact\"",

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-12 03:06:11 UTC)

I have audited the codebase and compiled a comprehensive report. You can review the full details in the newly created artifact:

👉 **[sophias_space_audit.md](file:///C:/Users/thoma/.gemini/antigravity/brain/519c5a72-c69e-4845-a55c-8f0d04101e15/sophias_space_audit.md)**

### 🔑 Key Findings & Recommendations

1. **Critical Thread Churn & Resource Leaks**: In **[SoundSynth.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/SoundSynth.kt#L119-L154)**, the app spawns a raw OS `Thread` and a fresh `AudioTrack` every time a sound effect (pop, whoosh, chime) is requested. 
   - *Impact*: High CPU overhead, risk of exceeding device-specific limits on concurrent `AudioTrack` instances (resulting in crashes), and resource leaks if playback fails.
   - *Fix*: Migrate to Android's native `SoundPool` API, which natively handles sound pre-decoding, volume scaling, and voice concurrency.
2. **Memory/Thread Safety**: 
   - The `tts` engine reference in **[NarrationManager.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/NarrationManager.kt#L24)** should be marked `@Volatile` to guarantee safe thread-visibility across callbacks.
   - Flow collection in **[MainActivity.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/MainActivity.kt#L31-L42)** is launched using `lifecycleScope.launch` and remains active even when the app is in the background. Collecting with `repeatOnLifecycle` is recommended.
3. **Physics & Mathematics Validation**:
   - The physics integrator (**[GravitySim.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/physics/GravitySim.kt)**) uses a robust **Symplectic Euler** scheme to ensure long-term orbit stability without energy drift.
   - Keplerian solving (**[Ephemeris.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/sky/Ephemeris.kt)**) converges cleanly with a fixed 8-step Newton-Raphson approximation.
4. **Unit Tests**:
   - All 11 unit tests covering core mathematical, astronomical, and tracking functionality successfully build and pass.

<summary><b>Call `write_to_file` - *"Writing port scanner script"*</b></summary>

{
  "ArtifactMetadata": "{\"ArtifactType\":\"other\",\"RequestFeedback\":false,\"Summary\":\"Asynchronous port scanner script to find the phone's dynamic wireless debugging port.\"}",
  "CodeContent": "\"import asyncio\\nimport sys\\n\\nasync def try_connect(ip, port):\\n    try:\\n        conn = asyncio.open_connection(ip, port)\\n        reader, writer = await asyncio.wait_for(conn, timeout=0.15)\\n        writer.close()\\n        await writer.wait_closed()\\n        print(f\\\"FOUND:{port}\\\", flush=True)\\n        return port\\n    except Exception:\\n        return None\\n\\nasync def main():\\n    ip = \\\"192.168.50.67\\\"\\n    start_port = 35000\\n    end_port = 45000\\n    \\n    print(f\\\"Scanning {ip} from {start_port} to {end_port}...\\\")\\n    tasks = []\\n    # Scan in batches to avoid OS resource limits\\n    batch_size = 500\\n    for i in range(start_port, end_port, batch_size):\\n        batch_tasks = [try_connect(ip, port) for port in range(i, min(i + batch_size, end_port))]\\n        results = await asyncio.gather(*batch_tasks)\\n        found = [r for r in results if r is not None]\\n        if found:\\n            print(f\\\"Scan complete. Active port: {found[0]}\\\")\\n            sys.exit(0)\\n            \\n    print(\\\"No open ports found.\\\")\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    asyncio.run(main())\\n\"",
  "Description": "\"Write python script to scan phone ports.\"",
  "IsArtifact": "false",
  "Overwrite": "true",
  "TargetFile": "\"C:\\\\Users\\\\thoma\\\\.gemini\\\\antigravity\\\\brain\\\\519c5a72-c69e-4845-a55c-8f0d04101e15\\\\scan.py\"",
  "toolAction": "\"Writing port scanner script\"",

Since the environment's sandbox cleans up background processes (like the ADB daemon) when a command block finishes, we need to run the `connect`, a short sleep, and the `install` command **together in a single execution block**.

Both the local debug APK and the website release APK are failing to install due to a signature mismatch with the version currently on your phone (`Failure [INSTALL_FAILED_UPDATE_INCOMPATIBLE]`). 

This signature mismatch happens because:
1. The app currently on your phone was likely signed with a production/release key (e.g., if it was installed from the Google Play Store or signed manually).
2. The local project's `build.gradle.kts` does not have a release signing configuration defined, meaning Gradle's default `debug` keystore is used for debug builds.

To get the updated local build onto your phone, we have two options:

### Option 1 (Recommended): Uninstall & Clean Install
We can uninstall the existing version on your phone and install the fresh debug build.
* ⚠️ **Warning**: This will reset the app data on your phone (such as Sophia's earned stickers).

  ```powershell
  $adb = "C:\Users\thoma\AppData\Local\Android\Sdk\platform-tools\adb.exe"
  & $adb -s 192.168.50.67:38001 uninstall com.example.sophiaspace
  & $adb -s 192.168.50.67:38001 install -r "c:\Users\thoma\Dropbox\My Documents\Programs\Solar System\app\app\build\outputs\apk\debug\app-debug.apk"
  ```

### Option 2: Keep current version
If you want to keep the stickers and progress on the phone, we would need the signing keystore file and configurations to sign the local APK with the matching key.

Which option would you like to proceed with?

## 👤 User Request (2026-06-12 03:26:48 UTC)

made changed (Two device-feedback fixes — a hover glow on constellations, and a real overhaul of the rocket launch feel. On the rocket, I know exactly why it's "weird and weak" from re-examining my own code: the hop physics runs at 0.25× time scale, which makes the entire flight last about a third of a second at only ~20% of Earth's radius (blink → parachute), the heading flips garbage at the apex where velocity ≈ 0, the orbit insertion teleports the rocket from the pad to the ring, and there's no flame or trail. Fixing all of it:


Edited 4 files, created a file, ran 3 commands
Both fixes are live in v1.3.3 (build 7) — SophiasSpace-1.3.3.apk on the family site, 55/55 tests green, copy hash-verified.

Constellation hover glow: the moment the reticle settles on a figure, all of its stars get a gold halo and its connect-the-dots lines fade in as a preview — both intensifying as the 1.5-second dwell ring fills. So she gets immediate "you found something, hold still!" feedback instead of staring at an unexplained timer, and the reveal animation still lands as the payoff.

Rocket launch — root causes found and fixed. Your "weird and weak" was four real defects stacking:

Weak: the hop ran at 0.25× time scale with a low launch speed — the entire flight lasted about a third of a second and rose only ~20% of Earth's radius. Now it's a slow-motion lob (~3 seconds in the air) arcing to ~57% of the planet's radius — it even sails just past the dashed orbit ring before the pull wins, which makes the lesson sharper.
Weird #1: at the top of the arc the rocket's nose snapped to a random direction (atan2 of near-zero velocity). Heading now freezes through the apex, then the rocket noses over and dives naturally.
Weird #2: the orbit outcome teleported the rocket from the pad onto the ring. It now flies a 1.4-second animated gravity-turn — straight up off the pad, visibly tipping over, merging tangentially onto the ring — and only then does the real simulation take the wheel for the loops.
Lifeless: added a flickering exhaust flame while the engine burns (ascent, or hop still rising), a breadcrumb trail so the whole arc stays visible, and a little parachute pop on deploy. The rocket also now stays where it landed downrange instead of snapping back to the pad.
The hop is still honest physics — a real ballistic arc in GravitySim, just slowed down and given more energy; only the gravity-turn ascent is choreographed, and it hands off to the true integrator at insertion.

Grab v1.3.3 from the site (or the Y: folder directly) and keep the feedback coming — this loop of you testing on-device and me turning fixes around is working well. Still curious what the install blocker turned out to be, in case it bites again on this update.) please push the new build to my phone

1: ---
2: title: "Sophia's Space — Product Requirements"
3: project: SophiaSpace
4: version: 1.0-draft

7: status: Approved for build
8: ---
9: 
10: # Sophia's Space — A Solar System for a 5-Year-Old
11: 
12: ## Executive statement
13: 
14: Sophia's Space is a fully narrated, exploratory Android app that lets a 5-year-old pre-reader *feel* how the solar system works — not by reading about it, but by spinning the Earth with her finger, dragging the Moon out of its orbit and watching it fly away, launching rockets that miss Mars until she learns to aim where Mars is *going to be*, and holding the phone up to the real night sky to find the constellations hiding there. Everything speaks. Nothing punishes. Every broken thing can be put back.
15: 
16: The big ideas, at her level:
17: 
18: | Big idea | How she experiences it |
19: |---|---|
20: | Everything in space is spinning and circling | Planets loop the Sun on glowing trails; she can speed time up and watch a year happen |
21: | A day is the Earth spinning | She spins Earth with her finger and watches her little house go from sunshine to stars |
22: | Moon phases are sunlight + shadow | She drags the Moon around Earth and a "what we see from home" window shows the phase change live |
23: | Gravity holds it all together — and has consequences | A real (simplified) gravity simulation: move Earth closer to the Sun and it sweats; fling the Moon and it escapes |
24: | Rockets curve, and missions aim ahead | Straight up falls back down; sideways-fa
<truncated 9763 bytes>
ll TTS narration, single shared engine | Pre-reader; fixes SophiaQuest audit L6 (per-screen engines) by design | Decided |
104: | DataStore only, no Room | Settings + progress flags only; no ledger-like data | Decided |
105: | Natural units (G·M☉ = 4π², AU, years) | Earth at r=1, v=2π orbits perfectly — zero tuning | Decided |
106: | No camera AR, no location permission | Permission-free manifest; city picked manually once | Superseded — see below |
107: | CAMERA permission for the optional "Real sky" AR mode | Tom's call after device testing (2026-06-11): CameraX preview under the existing star overlay; the look vector already WAS the camera axis. Frame never stored or transmitted; still no location/internet | Decided 2026-06-11 |
108: | Package `com.example.sophiaspace` | Matches `com.example.sophiaquest` style | Decided |

1: # Phase 4 — Sky View Implementation Plan
2: 
3: > **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
4: 
5: **Goal:** Build the point-at-the-sky module: real star positions, live planet/Moon ephemeris, drag mode first and sensor mode second, dwell-to-find constellations with stickers.
6: 
7: **Architecture:** Three pure-Kotlin units carry all the risk — `AstroMath` (time/coordinates/projection), `Ephemeris` (Keplerian planets + truncated lunar theory), and `SkyCatalog` (JSON star/constellation data) — each JUnit-tested with *structural* invariants (e.g., Polaris altitude ≈ observer latitude) rather than memorized almanac values. One `SkyViewScreen` consumes them; the existing `OrientationProvider` supplies sensor pointing; `MoonPhaseMath` + `litMoonPath` from Phase 2 draw tonight's real Moon.
8: 
9: **Tech Stack:** Kotlin, kotlinx-serialization-json (new dependency), Jetpack Compose Canvas, `OrientationProvider`, DataStore.
10: 
11: **Execution gates & ground rules:**
12: - ⛔ **Prerequisite:** the Phase-0 sensor spike must have been verified on the real Pixel (still unticked on the dashboard). Drag mode ships regardless; sensor mode depends on that spike.
13: - Depends on Phase 2 (`MoonPhaseMath`, `litMoonPath`).
14: - No git repo: "Commit" → ✅ **Checkpoint**. Recommend `git init`.
15: - 📐 **Catalog de-scope (decision for Tom):** v1 ships a curated ~5

1: # Phase 3 — Rocket Module Implementation Plan
2: 
3: > **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
4: 
5: **Goal:** Build the Rocket module: Lesson A "Up isn't enough" (gravity-turn toy) and Lesson B "Catch Mars" (aim-ahead interception, rigged to succeed by attempt 3–4).
6: 
7: **Architecture:** All rocket math is pure Kotlin and tested: `CatchMars` forward-simulates shots through a fresh `GravitySim` (the same engine powers the dotted preview, miss detection, AND the "aim here" hint — nothing is faked), `HintLadder` is the escalation state machine, and Lesson A's orbit insertion reuses `Scenario.settleIntoOrbit`. Two lesson composables sit under a tabbed `RocketScreen` host, mirroring the Sandbox's scenario tabs.
8: 
9: **Tech Stack:** Kotlin, Jetpack Compose Canvas, existing `GravitySim`/`Body`/`OrbitalMath`/`settleIntoOrbit`, `RevolutionTracker` (from Phase 2), JUnit4.
10: 
11: **Execution gates & ground rules:**
12: - Builds after Phase 2 (it reuses `RevolutionTracker` from `lessons/`). PRD allows Phase 3 to ship independently; GATE 2 observations may reorder priorities.
13: - No git repo: "Commit" → ✅ **Checkpoint** (all tests green). Recommend `git init` first.
14: - 🔒 Pedagogy guardrails: no fail-states (misses are gags with a waving ghost Mars, never losses); orbit framed as *"you keep missing the ground"*; never say "no gravity in space."

<truncated 35266 bytes>
ize.width / 2f, size.height / 2f)
773:             fun toScreen(wx: Double, wy: Double) =
774:                 Offset((c.x + wx * s).toFloat(), (c.y - wy * s).toFloat())
775: 
776:             // Sun + orbit guides.
777:             drawCircle(Brush.radialGradient(listOf(SunCore, SunGlow), c, s * 0.18f), s * 0.18f, c)
778:             drawCircle(StarGold.copy(alpha = 0.10f), (1.0 * s).toFloat(), c, style = Stroke(2f))
779:             drawCircle(StarGold.copy(alpha = 0.10f), (1.52 * s).toFloat(), c, style = Stroke(2f))
780:

782:                 shot else preview

784: 
785:             // Earth at its launch spot.
786:             drawCircle(EarthBlue, s * 0.09f, toScreen(CatchMars.EARTH_RADIUS, 0.0))
787: 
788:             // Mars: launch-time ghost + live position.

1: # Phase 2 — Guided Lessons Implementation Plan
2: 
3: > **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
4: 
5: **Goal:** Build the three guided-lesson screens — Orbits ("Spinning"), Day & Night, and Moon Phases — riding the existing physics engine and narration system.
6: 
7: **Architecture:** A new pure-Kotlin `lessons/` package holds all testable lesson math (phase geometry, day/night crossing detection, revolution counting). Three new Compose screens consume it, wired through the existing Navigation3 back stack and `ScriptKey` narration. No new dependencies.
8: 
9: **Tech Stack:** Kotlin, Jetpack Compose Canvas, existing `GravitySim`/`Scenario`, `NarrationManager`, DataStore stickers, JUnit4.
10: 
11: **Execution gates & ground rules:**
12: - ⛔ **Do not start until GATE 1 (sandbox kid test) is observed** — PRD hard rule.
13: - The project is **not a git repository**; "Commit" steps are replaced by ✅ **Checkpoint** steps (all tests green). Recommend `git init` before starting; if done, commit at each checkpoint.
14: - Pedagogy guardrails (from `docs/Teaching the Solar System…`) are **requirements**, not suggestions. They are called out inline as 🔒.
15: - All narration lines go in `Scripts.kt`, spoken in the friendly-star "we" voice. Lesson sequences use `Priority.ENQUEUE`; reactions use `INTERRUPT`.
16: 
17: **File structure (whole phase):**
18:

242:         }
243:     }
244: 
245:     fun reset() { wasDay = null }
246: }
247: ```

1: package com.example.sophiaspace.physics
2: 
3: import kotlin.math.hypot
4: 
5: /**
6:  * A celestial body in the toy gravity simulation.
7:  *
8:  * Units are "natural" per scenario: the dominant attractor has mass 1.0 and
9:  * G * (attractor mass) = 4 * PI^2, so a circular orbit at distance 1 has period 1.
10:  * Draw size is cartoon-scaled and fully decoupled from the physics.
11:  */
12: class Body(
13:     val id: String,
14:     val name: String,
15:     val mass: Double,
16:     var x: Double,
17:     var y: Double,
18:     var vx: Double,
19:     var vy: Double,
20:     /** Whether this body pulls on the others (Sun yes, Mercury no). */
21:     val attracts: Boolean = false,
22:     /** Cartoon draw radius in dp — NOT a physical size. */
23:     val drawRadius: Float = 12f,
24:     /** True while a finger owns this body; the integrator leaves it alone. */
25:     var kinematic: Boolean = false,
26:     /** Parked off-screen after drifting past the world bound. */
27:     var lostInSpace: Boolean = false
28: ) {
29:     fun speed(): Double = hypot(vx, vy)
30:     fun distanceTo(other: Body): Double = hypot(x - other.x, y - other.y)
31: 
32:     fun snapshot() = BodyState(x, y, vx, vy)
33: 
34:     fun restore(state: BodyState) {
35:         x = state.x; y = state.y; vx = state.vx; vy = state.vy
36:         kinematic = false
37:         lostInSpace = false
38:     }
39: }
40: 
41: /** Immutable position/velocity capture, used for scenario reset. */
42: data class BodyState(val x: Double, val y: Double, val vx: Double, val vy: Double)
43:

1: package com.example.sophiaspace.ui.components
2: 
3: import androidx.compose.foundation.Canvas
4: import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
5: import androidx.compose.runtime.LaunchedEffect
6: import androidx.compose.runtime.getValue
7: import androidx.compose.runtime.mutableFloatStateOf
8: import androidx.compose.runtime.remember
9: import androidx.compose.runtime.setValue
10: import androidx.compose.runtime.withFrameNanos
11: import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
12: import androidx.compose.ui.geometry.Offset
13: import androidx.compose.ui.geometry.Size
14: import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color
15: import com.example.sophiaspace.theme.IceBlue
16: import com.example.sophiaspace.theme.MeadowGreen
17: import com.example.sophiaspace.theme.NebulaPurple
18: import com.example.sophiaspace.theme.RocketCoral
19: import com.example.sophiaspace.theme.StarGold
20: import kotlin.math.cos
21: import kotlin.math.sin
22: import kotlin.random.Random
23: 
24: private class ConfettiBit(
25:     val startFx: Float,
26:     val angle: Float,
27:     val speed: Float,
28:     val size: Float,
29:     val color: Color,
30:     val spin: Float
31: )
32: 
33: /**
34:  * A short celebratory burst of star-colored confetti. Plays once per [trigger]
35:  * change (ignoring the initial value) and cleans itself up.
36:  */
37: @Composable
38: fun ConfettiOverlay(trigger: Int, modifier: Modifier = Modifier) {
39:     val palette = listOf(StarGold, IceBlue, NebulaPurple, RocketCoral, MeadowGreen)
40:     val bits = remember(trigger) {
41:         if (trigger == 0) emptyList() else {
42:             val rng = Random(trigger)
43:             List(70) {
44:                 ConfettiBit(
45:                     startFx = 0.2f + rng.nextFloat() * 0.6f,
46:                     angle = (-105f + rng.nextFloat() * 30f) * (Math.PI.toFloat() / 180f),
47:                     speed = 600f + rng.nextFloat() * 700f,
48:                     size = 6f + rng.nextFloat() * 9f,
49:                     color = palette[rng.nextInt(palette.size)],
50:                     spin = rng.nextFloat() * 8f - 4f
51:                 )
52:             }
53:         }
54:     }
55: 
56:     var t by remember(trigger) { mutableFloatStateOf(0f) }
57:     LaunchedEffect(trigger) {
58:         if (trigger == 0) return@LaunchedEffect
59:         t = 0f
60:         val start = System.nanoTime()
61:         while (t < DURATION) {
62:             withFrameNanos { now -> t = (now - start) / 1_000_000_000f }
63:         }
64:     }
65: 
66:     if (bits.isEmpty() || t >= DURATION) return
67: 
68:     Canvas(modifier = modifier) {
69:         val gravity = 1200f
70:         for (b in bits) {
71:             val x = b.startFx * size.width + cos(b.angle) * b.speed * t
72:             val y = size.height * 0.75f + sin(b.angle) * b.speed * t + 0.5f * gravity * t * t
73:             if (y > size.height) continue
74:             val fade = (1f - t / DURATION).coerceIn(0f, 1f)
75:             drawRect(
76:                 color = b.color.copy(alpha = fade),
77:                 topLeft = Offset(x, y),
78:                 size = Size(b.size, b.size * (0.6f + 0.4f * sin(b.spin * t * 6f)))
79:             )
80:         }
81:     }
82: }
83: 
84: private const val DURATION = 1.8f
85:

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: # Research Brief: Teaching the Solar System to Sophia (Age 5) — A Pedagogical & Narration Guide for a Narrated, Exploratory Android App
2: 
3: ## TL;DR
4: - A typical 5-year-old (Piaget's preoperational stage) can master **observable, embodied, single-variable patterns** — the Earth spinning to make day/night, the Moon changing shape, "everything goes around the Sun" — but cannot yet coordinate the two-frames-of-reference spatial reasoning needed for the *causal mechanisms* behind moon phases, seasons, and orbital intercept; design for the observable layer and plant honest seeds, not full mechanisms.
5: - The single biggest interactive-design hazard is that vivid, compressed, side-by-side visuals actively *manufacture* the most stubborn misconceptions in astronomy — the Earth's-shadow theory of moon phases, "planets are close together," and "seasons come from distance to the Sun"; the app must be engineered to avoid these, not merely to avoid mentioning them.
6: - Build on Hirsh-Pasek's four pillars (active, engaged, meaningful, socially interactive), embodied-cognition findings (Plummer's kinesthetic celestial-motion work; Lindgren & Johnson-Glenberg), and a warm first-person-plural "we" voice; keep sessions short (~10–15 min), reversible, and fear-free, and test comprehension through prediction and gesture, not verbal recall.
7: 
8: ## Key Findings
9: 
10: **1. Developmental ceiling is real and specific.** At age 5 Sophia is in Piaget's preoperational stage: symboli
<truncated 34633 bytes>
ified against the paywalled Plummer & Maynard (2014) primary text; the level *structure* (Earth-based → coordinated frames) is corroborated across multiple academic sources, and the "spatial reasoning as the dimension of progress" framing is verbatim from Plummer (2014).
203: - **Emotional-design specifics** for fear-reduction in *science* apps draw partly from adjacent literatures (children's mental-health apps; child-AI interaction design); direct RCT evidence for this exact use case is limited.
204: 
205: ### Highest-leverage principles (apply app-wide)
206: 1. **Embodiment-congruent gestures** — make the finger-motion *be* the concept.
207: 2. **Observe before explain** — teach the Earth-based observable layer; defer mechanisms.
208: 3. **Total reversibility & zero fail-states** — undo everything; reframe "failure" as "try again."
209: 4. **Avoid manufacturing misconceptions** — engineer visuals against the shadow-phase, close-planets, and distance-seasons errors.
210: 5. **Simplify scope, never direction** — only teach things she won't have to unlearn.
211: 6. **Warm "we" companion voice** — discover *with* her; questions, not lectures.
212: 7. **Probe understanding via prediction & gesture**, not verbal recall.
213: 
214: ### Top pitfalls to avoid
215: 1. Earth's-shadow / creeping-disc imagery for moon phases (the #1 manufactured misconception).
216: 2. "Closer to the Sun = summer," or any distance-based seasons framing.
217: 3. "The Sun goes down / goes to sleep / hides" (geocentric + animism).
218: 4. "No gravity in space" (contradicts orbit-as-falling).
219: 5. Rubber-sheet gravity and other 2-D abstractions she can't decode.
220: 6. Big numbers ("93 million miles") as the vehicle for scale — she can't represent them.
221: 7. Top-down orrery as the *only* view (disconnects from her ground-level sky).
222: 8. Scary vastness/emptiness framing; narrator-paced timing she can't control; punishing failure sounds.
The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

1: ---
2: title: "Sophia's Space — Product Requirements"
3: project: SophiaSpace
4: version: 1.0-draft
5: date: 2026-06-09
6: author: Tom (with Claude)
7: status: Approved for build
8: ---
9: 
10: # Sophia's Space — A Solar System for a 5-Year-Old
11: 
12: ## Executive statement
13: 
14: Sophia's Space is a fully narrated, exploratory Android app that lets a 5-year-old pre-reader *feel* how the solar system works — not by reading about it, but by spinning the Earth with her finger, dragging the Moon out of its orbit and watching it fly away, launching rockets that miss Mars until she learns to aim where Mars is *going to be*, and holding the phone up to the real night sky to find the constellations hiding there. Everything speaks. Nothing punishes. Every broken thing can be put back.
15: 
16: The big ideas, at her level:
17: 
18: | Big idea | How she experiences it |
19: |---|---|
20: | Everything in space is spinning and circling | Planets loop the Sun on glowing trails; she can speed time up and watch a year happen |
21: | A day is the Earth spinning | She spins Earth with her finger and watches her little house go from sunshine to stars |
22: | Moon phases are sunlight + shadow | She drags the Moon around Earth and a "what we see from home" window shows the phase change live |
23: | Gravity holds it all together — and has consequences | A real (simplified) gravity simulation: move Earth closer to the Sun and it sweats; fling the Moon and it escapes |
24: | Rockets curve, and missions aim ahead | Straight up falls back down; sideways-fa
<truncated 9763 bytes>
ll TTS narration, single shared engine | Pre-reader; fixes SophiaQuest audit L6 (per-screen engines) by design | Decided |
104: | DataStore only, no Room | Settings + progress flags only; no ledger-like data | Decided |
105: | Natural units (G·M☉ = 4π², AU, years) | Earth at r=1, v=2π orbits perfectly — zero tuning | Decided |
106: | No camera AR, no location permission | Permission-free manifest; city picked manually once | Superseded — see below |
107: | CAMERA permission for the optional "Real sky" AR mode | Tom's call after device testing (2026-06-11): CameraX preview under the existing star overlay; the look vector already WAS the camera axis. Frame never stored or transmitted; still no location/internet | Decided 2026-06-11 |
108: | Package `com.example.sophiaspace` | Matches `com.example.sophiaquest` style | Decided |
109: 
110: ## Open questions
111: 
112: | Question | Recommendation / next step |
113: |---|---|
114: | Narrator character — a friendly star? A little astronaut Sophia? | ✅ RESOLVED 2026-06-10: little astronaut 👩‍🚀 |
115: | Should the Sun playground include Uranus/Neptune? | ✅ RESOLVED 2026-06-10: in the Night Sky (live ephemeris, subtle dots); playground stays Mercury→Mars — honest spacing can't fit the giants in portrait |
116: | Default home city for the sky math | ✅ RESOLVED 2026-06-10: West Orange, NJ built-in default; 25-city picker in the grown-up sheet |
117: | Sticker art style — emoji-based v1 or custom Canvas drawings? | ✅ RESOLVED 2026-06-10: emoji shelf shipped on Home; revisit custom art after GATE 2 |
118: | Does Catch Mars need a difficulty beyond the rigged ramp? | No for v1 — observe at GATE 2/3 whether she replays it |

1: # Phase 2 — Guided Lessons Implementation Plan
2: 
3: > **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
4: 
5: **Goal:** Build the three guided-lesson screens — Orbits ("Spinning"), Day & Night, and Moon Phases — riding the existing physics engine and narration system.
6: 
7: **Architecture:** A new pure-Kotlin `lessons/` package holds all testable lesson math (phase geometry, day/night crossing detection, revolution counting). Three new Compose screens consume it, wired through the existing Navigation3 back stack and `ScriptKey` narration. No new dependencies.
8: 
9: **Tech Stack:** Kotlin, Jetpack Compose Canvas, existing `GravitySim`/`Scenario`, `NarrationManager`, DataStore stickers, JUnit4.
10: 
11: **Execution gates & ground rules:**
12: - ⛔ **Do not start until GATE 1 (sandbox kid test) is observed** — PRD hard rule.
13: - The project is **not a git repository**; "Commit" steps are replaced by ✅ **Checkpoint** steps (all tests green). Recommend `git init` before starting; if done, commit at each checkpoint.
14: - Pedagogy guardrails (from `docs/Teaching the Solar System…`) are **requirements**, not suggestions. They are called out inline as 🔒.
15: - All narration lines go in `Scripts.kt`, spoken in the friendly-star "we" voice. Lesson sequences use `Priority.ENQUEUE`; reactions use `INTERRUPT`.
16: 
17: **File structure (whole phase):**
18:

204: 
205: /**
206:  * Path of the LIT part of the moon's face — the standard two-arc phase shape:
207:  * half-circle outer limb on the lit side + semi-elliptical terminator whose
208:  * half-width is r*|2f-1|, bowing toward the lit side for crescents and away
209:  * for gibbous. Painting light ON TOP of a dim base means no shadow object can
210:  * ever appear — a pedagogy requirement (research pitfall #1), not a style choice.
211:  */
212: fun litMoonPath(cx: Float, cy: Float, r: Float, fraction: Float, litOnRight: Boolean): Path {
213:     val path = Path()
214:     val f = fraction.coerceIn(0f, 1f)
215:     if (f <= 0.01f) return path
216:     if (f >= 0.99f) {
217:         path.addOval(Rect(cx - r, cy - r, cx + r, cy + r))
218:         return path
219:     }
220:     val side = if (litOnRight) 1f else -1f
221:     // Outer limb: top -> bottom along the lit side.
222:     path.moveTo(cx, cy - r)
223:     path.arcTo(
224:         Rect(cx - r, cy - r, cx + r, cy + r),
225:         startAngleDegrees = -90f, sweepAngleDegrees = side * 180f, forceMoveTo = false
226:     )
227:     // Terminator: bottom -> top along a half-ellipse of semi-width a.
228:     val a = (r * abs(2f * f - 1f)).coerceAtLeast(0.5f)
229:     val sweep = if (f < 0.5f) -side * 180f else side * 180f
230:     path.arcTo(
231:         Rect(cx - a, cy - r, cx + a, cy + r),
232:         startAngleDegrees = 90f, sweepAngleDegrees = sweep, forceMoveTo = false
233:     )
234:     path.close()
235:     return path
236: }
237:

1: # Phase 3 — Rocket Module Implementation Plan
2: 
3: > **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
4: 
5: **Goal:** Build the Rocket module: Lesson A "Up isn't enough" (gravity-turn toy) and Lesson B "Catch Mars" (aim-ahead interception, rigged to succeed by attempt 3–4).
6: 
7: **Architecture:** All rocket math is pure Kotlin and tested: `CatchMars` forward-simulates shots through a fresh `GravitySim` (the same engine powers the dotted preview, miss detection, AND the "aim here" hint — nothing is faked), `HintLadder` is the escalation state machine, and Lesson A's orbit insertion reuses `Scenario.settleIntoOrbit`. Two lesson composables sit under a tabbed `RocketScreen` host, mirroring the Sandbox's scenario tabs.
8: 
9: **Tech Stack:** Kotlin, Jetpack Compose Canvas, existing `GravitySim`/`Body`/`OrbitalMath`/`settleIntoOrbit`, `RevolutionTracker` (from Phase 2), JUnit4.
10: 
11: **Execution gates & ground rules:**
12: - Builds after Phase 2 (it reuses `RevolutionTracker` from `lessons/`). PRD allows Phase 3 to ship independently; GATE 2 observations may reorder priorities.
13: - No git repo: "Commit" → ✅ **Checkpoint** (all tests green). Recommend `git init` first.
14: - 🔒 Pedagogy guardrails: no fail-states (misses are gags with a waving ghost Mars, never losses); orbit framed as *"you keep missing the ground"*; never say "no gravity in space."

778:             drawCircle(StarGold.copy(alpha = 0.10f), (1.0 * s).toFloat(), c, style = Stroke(2f))
779:             drawCircle(StarGold.copy(alpha = 0.10f), (1.52 * s).toFloat(), c, style = Stroke(2f))

14: /**
15:  * Tiny 8-bit-style synthesizer for interaction juice (SophiaQuest lineage).
16:  *
17:  * The six effects are rendered to small WAV files in the cache dir once, then
18:  * played through a single shared [SoundPool] — which owns the thread/voice
19:  * pool, enforces a concurrency cap, and applies volume live. (The previous
20:  * thread-per-sound AudioTrack scheme could exhaust the device's AudioTrack
21:  * limit under a five-year-old's tap rate, and leaked tracks on error paths.)
22:  *
23:  * The NarrationManager ducks this while speaking via [volumeScale].
24:  */
25: object SoundSynth {
26:     private const val SAMPLE_RATE = 22050
27: 
28:     @Volatile
29:     var volumeScale: Float = 1.0f
30: 
31:     private enum class Effect { POP, WHOOSH, SIZZLE, DRIFT, CHIME, FANFARE }
32: 
33:     @Volatile
34:     private var pool: SoundPool? = null
35:     private val soundIds = ConcurrentHashMap<Effect, Int>()
36:     private val readySamples = ConcurrentHashMap<Int, Boolean>()
37: 
38:     /** Call once from MainActivity.onCreate. Idempotent; renders off-thread. */
39:     fun init(context: Context) {
40:
<truncated 5644 bytes>

176:         }
177:         return buffer
178:     }
179: 
180:     private fun renderFanfare(): ShortArray {
181:         val numSamples = samples(600)
182:         val buffer = ShortArray(numSamples)
183:         for (i in 0 until numSamples) {
184:             val t = i.toDouble() / SAMPLE_RATE
185:             val noteIdx = i / (numSamples / 4)
186:             val freq = when (noteIdx) {
187:                 0 -> 523.25
188:                 1 -> 783.99
189:                 2 -> 659.25
190:                 else -> 1046.50
191:             }
192:             val vibrato = if (noteIdx == 3) sin(2.0 * PI * 12.0 * t) * 8.0 else 0.0
193:             val envelope = 1.0 - (i.toDouble() / numSamples)
194:             buffer[i] = (sin(2.0 * PI * (freq + vibrato) * t) * 12000.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
195:         }
196:         return buffer
197:     }
198: 
199:     private fun samples(durationMs: Int) = (SAMPLE_RATE * (durationMs / 1000.0)).toInt()
200: 
201:     /** Minimal 16-bit mono PCM WAV writer. */
202:     private fun writeWav(file: File, pcm: ShortArray) {
203:         DataOutputStream(BufferedOutputStream(FileOutputStream(file))).use { o ->
204:             fun le32(v: Int) {
205:                 o.write(v and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 8) and 0xFF)
206:                 o.write((v shr 16) and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 24) and 0xFF)
207:             }
208:             fun le16(v: Int) {
209:                 o.write(v and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 8) and 0xFF)
210:             }
211:             val dataSize = pcm.size * 2
212:             o.writeBytes("RIFF"); le32(36 + dataSize); o.writeBytes("WAVE")
213:             o.writeBytes("fmt "); le32(16); le16(1); le16(1)
214:             le32(SAMPLE_RATE); le32(SAMPLE_RATE * 2); le16(2); le16(16)
215:             o.writeBytes("data"); le32(dataSize)
216:             for (s in pcm) le16(s.toInt() and 0xFFFF)
217:         }
218:     }
219: }

12: /**
13:  * The app's single shared voice. One TextToSpeech engine for every screen,
14:  * warmed up at launch; requests issued before init completes are buffered.
15:  *
16:  * Priorities:
17:  *  - [Priority.INTERRUPT] flushes whatever is being said (consequences, taps).
18:  *  - [Priority.ENQUEUE] waits its turn (lesson sequences).
19:  */
20: class NarrationManager(context: Context) {
21: 
22:     enum class Priority { INTERRUPT, ENQUEUE }
23: 
24:     @Volatile
25:     private var tts: TextToSpeech? = null
26: 
27:     @Volatile
28:     private var ready = false
29:     private val pending = ArrayDeque<Pair<String, Priority>>()
30:     private val utteranceSeq = AtomicInteger(0)
31: 
32:     private val _isSpeaking = MutableStateFlow(false)
33: 
34:     /** Drives the narrator character's glow/pulse. */
35:     val isSpeaking: StateFlow<Boolean> = _isSpeaking
36: 
37:     /** 0..1, applied per-utterance. Wired to the grown-up settings slider. */
38:     @Volatile
39:     var volume: Float = 1.0f
40: 
41:     /** TTS speech rate. Slightly slow by default — the listener is five. */
42:     @Volatile
43:     var ra

74:                         speakNow(text, priority)
75:                     }
76:                 }

80: 
81:     fun speak(key: ScriptKey, priority: Priority = Priority.INTERRUPT) {
82:         speakText(key.line, priority)
83:     }
84: 
85:     fun speakText(text: String, priority: Priority = Priority.INTERRUPT) {
86:         if (!ready) {
87:             synchronized(pending) {
88:                 if (!ready) { // re-check under the lock: init may have just drained
89:                     if (priority == Priority.INTERRUPT) pending.clear()
90:                     pending.addLast(text to priority)
91:                     return
92:                 }
93:             }
94:         }
95:         speakNow(text, priority)
96:     }
97: 
98:     private fun speakNow(text: String, priority: Priority) {
99:         val engine = tts ?: return
100:         engine.setSpeechRate(rate)
101:         val params = Bundle().apply {
102:             putFloat(TextToSpeech.Engine.KEY_PARAM_VOLUME, volume)
103:         }
104:         val mode = if (priority == Priority.INTERRUPT) TextToSpeech.QUEUE_FLUSH else TextToSpeech.QUEUE_ADD
105:         engine.speak(text, mode, params, "sophiaspace-${utteranceSeq.incrementAndGet()}")
106:     }
107: 
108:     /** Stop talking immediately (navigation, app pause). */
109:     fun stop() {
110:         tts?.stop()
111:         _isSpeaking.value = false
112:     }
113: 
114:     /** Release the engine. Call from Activity.onDestroy only. */
115:     fun shutdown() {
116:         tts?.stop()
117:         tts?.shutdown()
118:         tts = null
119:         ready = false
120:     }
121: }

1: # Phase 4 — Sky View Implementation Plan
2: 
3: > **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
4: 
5: **Goal:** Build the point-at-the-sky module: real star positions, live planet/Moon ephemeris, drag mode first and sensor mode second, dwell-to-find constellations with stickers.
6: 
7: **Architecture:** Three pure-Kotlin units carry all the risk — `AstroMath` (time/coordinates/projection), `Ephemeris` (Keplerian planets + truncated lunar theory), and `SkyCatalog` (JSON star/constellation data) — each JUnit-tested with *structural* invariants (e.g., Polaris altitude ≈ observer latitude) rather than memorized almanac values. One `SkyViewScreen` consumes them; the existing `OrientationProvider` supplies sensor pointing; `MoonPhaseMath` + `litMoonPath` from Phase 2 draw tonight's real Moon.
8: 
9: **Tech Stack:** Kotlin, kotlinx-serialization-json (new dependency), Jetpack Compose Canvas, `OrientationProvider`, DataStore.
10: 
11: **Execution gates & ground rules:**
12: - ⛔ **Prerequisite:** the Phase-0 sensor spike must have been verified on the real Pixel (still unticked on the dashboard). Drag mode ships regardless; sensor mode depends on that spike.
13: - Depends on Phase 2 (`MoonPhaseMath`, `litMoonPath`).
14: - No git repo: "Commit" → ✅ **Checkpoint**. Recommend `git init`.
15: - 📐 **Catalog de-scope (decision for Tom):** v1 ships a curated ~5

770: @Composable
771: fun SkyViewScreen(
772:     onBack: () -> Unit,
773:     narrator: NarrationManager,
774:     settings: AppSettings,
775:     modifier: Modifier = Modifier
776: ) {
777:     val context = LocalContext.current
778:     val catalog = remember { SkyCatalog.loadFromAssets(context) }
779:     val orientation = remember { OrientationProvider(context) }
780:     val lat by settings.homeLatitude.collectAsState(initial = 40f)
781:     val lon by settings.homeLongitude.collectAsState(initial = -75f)
782:     val found by settings.foundConstellations.collectAsState(initial = emptySet())
783: 
784:     var sensorMode by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
785:     var dragAlt by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(35f) }
786:     var dragAz by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(180f) }
787:     val pointing by orientation.pointing.collectAsState()
788:     var frameTick by remember { mutableLongStateOf(0L) }
789:     var confetti by remember { mutableIntStateOf(0) }
790:     var dwellTargetId by remember { mutableStateOf<String?>(null) }
791:     var dwellStartNs by remember { mutableLongStateOf(0L) }
792:     var revealId by remember { mutableStateOf<String?>(null) }
793:     var revealProgress by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(0f) }

28:     }

32:         val lstDeg = gmstDeg(jd) + lonDeg
33:         val h = Math.toRadians(((lstDeg - raDeg) % 360.0 + 360.0) % 360.0)
34:         val dec = Math.toRadians(decDeg)
35:         val lat = Math.toRadians(latDeg)

37:         val alt = asin(sinAlt.coerceIn(-1

73:         val forward = s.first * f.first + s.second * f.second + s.third * f.third

77:     /**
78:      * Stereographic projection of (alt, az) about a look-center, returning
79:      * tangent-plane coordinates (x grows toward the right of the view,
80:      * y up), or null when the point is behind the camera (cull). Used for
81:      * the drawn-sky modes — pleasing wide-angle look.
82:      */
83:     fun project(altDeg: Double, azDeg: Double, centerAltDeg: Double, centerAzDeg: Double): Pair<Double, Double>? {
84:         val (right, up, forward) = cameraFrame(altDeg, azDeg, centerAltDeg, centerAzDeg)
85:         if (forward <= 0.05) return null // behind / >87° away
86:         val k = 2.0 / (1.0 + forward)
87:         return (k * right) to (k * up)
88:     }
89: 
90:     /**
91:      * Rectilinear (gnomonic) projection — what a camera LENS does. Returns
92:      * tan-angle offsets; multiply by the focal length in pixels and the
93:      * overlay pans at exactly the camera image's rate. AR mode only.
94:      */
95:     fun projectCamera(altDeg: Double, azDeg: Double, centerAltDeg: Double, centerAzDeg: Double): Pair<Double, Double>? {
96:         val (right, up, forward) = cameraFrame(altDeg, azDeg, centerAltDeg, centerAzDeg)
97:         if (forward <= 0.15) return null // rectilinear blows up near 90° off-axis
98:         return (right / forward) to (up / forward)
99:     }
100: 
101:     /** Great-circle separation between two (alt, az) directions, degrees. */
102:     fun angularSeparationDeg(alt1: Double, az1: Double, alt2: Double, az2: Double): Double {
103:         val a1 = Math.toRadians(alt1)
104:         val a2 = Math.toRadians(alt2)
105:         val dAz = Math.toRadians(az1 - az2)
106:         val cosSep = sin(a1) * sin(a2) + cos(a1) * cos(a2) * cos(dAz)
107:         return Math.toDegrees(acos(cosSep.coerceIn(-1.0, 1.0)))
108:     }

13: 
14: /** Where the back of the phone is pointing, in sky coordinates. */
15: data class Pointing(
16:     /** Degrees clockwise from magnetic north, 0..360. */
17:     val azimuthDeg: Float,
18:     /** Degrees above the horizon, -90..90. */
19:     val altitudeDeg: Float
20: )
21: 
22: /**
23:  * Rotation-vector sensor → "magic window" look direction.
24:  *
25:  * The look vector is the device's -Z axis (out the back of the phone) expressed
26:  * in world ENU coordinates: hold the phone up like a window and the reported
27:  * azimuth/altitude is the patch of sky behind it. The raw vector is smoothed
28:  * with an exponential moving average and re-normalized — smoothing Euler angles
29:  * directly causes the classic 359°→0° snap.
30:  *
31:  * Register in onResume / unregister in onPause; the sensor must never run
32:  * while the screen is off (battery).
33:  */
34: class OrientationProvider(context: Context) : SensorEventListener {
35: 
36:     private val sensorManager = context.getSystemService(Context.SENSOR_SERVICE) as SensorManager
37:     private val rotationSensor
<truncated 1089 bytes>
5: 
66:     fun stop() {
67:         sensorManager.unregisterListener(this)
68:         primed = false
69:         _pointing.value = null
70:     }
71: 
72:     override fun onSensorChanged(event: SensorEvent) {
73:         if (event.sensor.type != Sensor.TYPE_ROTATION_VECTOR) return
74:         SensorManager.getRotationMatrixFromVector(rotationMatrix, event.values)
75: 
76:         // World-frame direction of the device -Z axis (out the back of the phone).
77:         // getRotationMatrixFromVector returns R with world = R * device, row-major.
78:         val lookX = -rotationMatrix[2]
79:         val lookY = -rotationMatrix[5]
80:         val lookZ = -rotationMatrix[8]
81: 
82:         if (!primed) {
83:             smoothX = lookX; smoothY = lookY; smoothZ = lookZ
84:             primed = true
85:         } else {
86:             val k = smoothing
87:             smoothX += k * (lookX - smoothX)
88:             smoothY += k * (lookY - smoothY)
89:             smoothZ += k * (lookZ - smoothZ)
90:         }
91:         val len = sqrt(smoothX * smoothX + smoothY * smoothY + smoothZ * smoothZ)
92:         if (len < 1e-6f) return
93:         val nx = smoothX / len
94:         val ny = smoothY / len
95:         val nz = smoothZ / len
96: 
97:         // World frame: X = east, Y = magnetic north, Z = up.
98:         val azimuth = Math.toDegrees(atan2(nx.toDouble(), ny.toDouble())).toFloat().let {
99:             if (it < 0f) it + 360f else it
100:         }
101:         val altitude = Math.toDegrees(asin(nz.toDouble().coerceIn(-1.0, 1.0))).toFloat()
102: 
103:         _pointing.value = Pointing(azimuth, altitude)
104:     }
105: 
106:     override fun onAccuracyChanged(sensor: Sensor?, accuracy: Int) = Unit
107:

112:     }

1: package com.example.sophiaspace.physics
2: 
3: import kotlin.math.hypot
4: 
5: /**
6:  * A celestial body in the toy gravity simulation.
7:  *
8:  * Units are "natural" per scenario: the dominant attractor has mass 1.0 and
9:  * G * (attractor mass) = 4 * PI^2, so a circular orbit at distance 1 has period 1.
10:  * Draw size is cartoon-scaled and fully decoupled from the physics.
11:  */
12: class Body(
13:     val id: String,
14:     val name: String,
15:     val mass: Double,
16:     var x: Double,
17:     var y: Double,
18:     var vx: Double,
19:     var vy: Double,
20:     /** Whether this body pulls on the others (Sun yes, Mercury no). */
21:     val attracts: Boolean = false,
22:     /** Cartoon draw radius in dp — NOT a physical size. */
23:     val drawRadius: Float = 12f,
24:     /** True while a finger owns this body; the integrator leaves it alone. */
25:     var kinematic: Boolean = false,
26:     /** Parked off-screen after drifting past the world bound. */
27:     var lostInSpace: Boolean = false
28: ) {
29:     fun speed(): Double = hypot(vx, vy)
30:     fun distanceTo(other: Body): Double = hypot(x - other.x, y - other.y)
31: 
32:     fun snapshot() = BodyState(x, y, vx, vy)
33: 
34:     fun restore(state: BodyState) {
35:         x = state.x; y = state.y; vx = state.vx; vy = state.vy
36:         kinematic = false
37:         lostInSpace = false
38:     }
39: }
40: 
41: /** Immutable position/velocity capture, used for scenario reset. */
42: data class BodyState(val x: Double, val y: Double, val vx: Double, val vy: Double)
