1: package com.example.sophiaspace.audio
2: 
3: /**
4:  * Every spoken line in the app lives here, keyed — one place to edit, soften,
5:  * or someday swap for parent-recorded voiceover. Lines are short and rhythmic
6:  * because the listener is five.
7:  */
8: enum class ScriptKey(val line: String) {
9:     // Home
10:     HOME_WELCOME("Welcome to space, Sophia! Tap somewhere to explore!"),
11:     HOME_SANDBOX("The playground! You can move the planets yourself!"),
12:     HOME_COMING_SOON("We're still building this one. Soon, I promise!"),
13:     HOME_ORBITS("Spinning! Watch everybody circle the Sun, around and around!"),
14:     HOME_DAY_NIGHT("Day and night! Give the Earth a spin with your finger!"),
15:     HOME_MOON("The Moon! Let's see why it looks different every night."),
16: 
17:     // Orbits lesson
18:     ORBITS_INTRO("Everybody is going around the Sun — around and around, like a big dance! Slide the magic clock to make time go faster."),
19:     ORBITS_YEAR_DONE("Look! Earth went all the way around the Sun. That's one whole year — one more birthday for you!"),
20:     ORBITS_SPIN_MARKER("See the little dot on Earth? Earth is spinning like a top WHILE it goes around. Spinning makes days. Going around makes years!"),
21:     ORBITS_FAST("Wheee, time is zooming! Watch how the close planets zip around fastest."),
22: 
23:     // Day & Night lesson — "WE spin", the Sun never moves or sleeps.
24:     DAYNIGHT_INTRO("Here's our Earth, and here's our little house. Put your finger on Earth and giv
<truncated 2536 bytes>
t there in your real sky!"),
50:     SKY_MOON_TAP("That's our Moon — looking exactly like it does outside tonight! Go check!"),
51: 
52:     // Sandbox — framing
53:     SANDBOX_EARTH_MOON_INTRO("Let's zoom way, way in! Here's our Earth, and our Moon going around it. Try dragging the Moon with your finger!"),
54:     SANDBOX_SUN_INTRO("Here's the Sun and its family! Everybody is circling the Sun. Try dragging a planet and see what happens!"),
55:     SANDBOX_GRABBED("Whee! You've got it! Where will you put it?"),
56:     SANDBOX_NEW_ORBIT("Look at that! A whole new path. Closer to the middle means faster — farther means slower!"),
57: 
58:     // Sandbox — consequences
59:     CONSEQUENCE_ESCAPED("Oh no! It's flying away into space! Gravity can't hold it anymore. Can you put it back?"),
60:     CONSEQUENCE_CRASHED("Sizzle! Too close! It fell right in. Let's put it back where it belongs."),
61:     CONSEQUENCE_LOST("It's so far away now, it's lost in space! Press the big button to bring it home."),
62:     // Habitable-zone framing on purpose: "lived / always", never weather or
63:     // season words — guards the "closer = summer" misconception (research brief).
64:     CONSEQUENCE_TOO_HOT("If Earth lived this close to the Sun, it would always be too hot for us! See it sweating? Let's find it a comfy spot."),
65:     CONSEQUENCE_TOO_COLD("If Earth lived way out here, it would always be too cold. Everything would freeze! See it shivering? Let's bring it home."),
66:     CONSEQUENCE_WOBBLY("Look! Its path is all squished now, like an egg. What a wobbly year that would be!"),
67:     CONSEQUENCE_RESTORED("You did it! Everything is back where it belongs. The sky is happy again!"),
68: 
69:     // Put it back
70:     PUT_IT_BACK("Okay! Watch everything float back home."),
71: 
72:     // Settings / grown-up — spoken when the kid taps the gear.

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: package com.example.sophiaspace.ui.components
2: 
3: import androidx.compose.foundation.Canvas
4: import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
5: import androidx.compose.runtime.LaunchedEffect
6: import androidx.compose.runtime.getValue
7: import androidx.compose.runtime.mutableFloatStateOf
8: import androidx.compose.runtime.remember
9: import androidx.compose.runtime.setValue
10: import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
11: import androidx.compose.ui.geometry.Offset
12: import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color
13: import kotlin.math.sin
14: import kotlin.random.Random
15: 
16: private class TwinkleStar(val fx: Float, val fy: Float, val size: Float, val phase: Float, val speed: Float)
17: 
18: /**
19:  * Gently twinkling star backdrop drawn behind every screen.
20:  * Stars are generated once per composition; only their alpha animates.
21:  */
22: @Composable
23: fun SpaceBackground(modifier: Modifier = Modifier, starCount: Int = 90) {
24:     val stars = remember {
25:         val rng = Random(42)
26:         List(starCount) {
27:             TwinkleStar(
28:                 fx = rng.nextFloat(),
29:                 fy = rng.nextFloat(),
30:                 size = 1.2f + rng.nextFloat() * 2.2f,
31:                 phase = rng.nextFloat() * 6.2832f,
32:                 speed = 0.4f + rng.nextFloat() * 0.9f
33:             )
34:         }
35:     }
36: 
37:     var time by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(0f) }
38:     LaunchedEffect(Unit) {
39:         val start = System.nanoTime()
40:         while (true) {
41:             androidx.compose.runtime.withFrameNanos { now ->
42:                 time = (now - start) / 1_000_000_000f
43:             }
44:         }
45:     }
46: 
47:     Canvas(modifier = modifier) {
48:         for (s in stars) {
49:             val alpha = 0.25f + 0.55f * (0.5f + 0.5f * sin(time * s.speed + s.phase))
50:             drawCircle(
51:                 color = Color.White.copy(alpha = alpha),
52:                 radius = s.size,
53:                 center = Offset(s.fx * size.width, s.fy * size.height)
54:             )
55:         }
56:     }
57: }

3: import androidx.compose.foundation.Canvas
4: import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
5: import androidx.compose.runtime.LaunchedEffect
6: import androidx.compose.runtime.getValue
7: import androidx.compose.runtime.mutableFloatStateOf
8: import androidx.compose.runtime.remember
9: import androidx.compose.runtime.setValue
10: import androidx.compose.runtime.withFrameNanos
11: import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
12: import androidx.compose.ui.geometry.Offset
13: import androidx.compose.ui.geometry.Size
14: import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color
15: import com.example.sophiaspace.theme.IceBlue
16: import com.example.sophiaspace.theme.MeadowGreen
17: import com.example.sophiaspace.theme.NebulaPurple
18: import com.example.sophiaspace.theme.RocketCoral
19: import com.example.sophiaspace.theme.StarGold
20: import kotlin.math.cos
21: import kotlin.math.sin
22: import kotlin.random.Random
23: 
24: private class ConfettiBit(
25:     val startFx: Float,
26:     val angle: Float,
27:     val speed: Float,
28:     val size: Float,
29:     val color: Color,
30:     val spin: Float
31: )
32: 
33: /**
34:  * A short celebratory burst of star-colored confetti. Plays once per [trigger]
35:  * change (ignoring the initial value) and cleans itself up.
36:  */
37: @Composable
38: fun ConfettiOverlay(trigger: Int, modifier: Modifier = Modifier) {
39:     val palette = listOf(StarGold, IceBlue, NebulaPurple, RocketCoral, MeadowGreen)
40:     val bits = remember(trigger) {
41:         if (trigger == 0) emptyList() else {
42:             val rng = Random(trigger)
43:             List(70) {
44:                 ConfettiBit(
45:                     startFx = 0.2f + rng.nextFloat() * 0.6f,
46:                     angle = (-105f + rng.nextFloat() * 30f) * (Math.PI.toFloat() / 180f),
47:                     speed = 600f + rng.nextFloat() * 700f,
48:                     size = 6f + rng.nextFloat() * 9f,
49:                     color = palette[rng.nextInt(palette.size)],
50:                     spin = rng.nextFloat() * 8f - 4f
51:                 )
52:             }
53:         }
54:     }
55: 
56:     var t by remember(trigger) { mutableFloatStateOf(0f) }
57:     LaunchedEffect(trigger) {
58:         if (trigger == 0) return@LaunchedEffect
59:         t = 0f
60:         val start = System.nanoTime()
61:         while (t < DURATION) {
62:             withFrameNanos { now -> t = (now - start) / 1_000_000_000f }
63:         }
64:     }
65: 
66:     if (bits.isEmpty() || t >= DURATION) return
67: 
68:     Canvas(modifier = modifier) {
69:         val gravity = 1200f
70:         for (b in bits) {
71:             val x = b.startFx * size.width + cos(b.angle) * b.speed * t
72:             val y = size.height * 0.75f + sin(b.angle) * b.speed * t + 0.5f * gravity * t * t
73:             if (y > size.height) continue
74:             val fade = (1f - t / DURATION).coerceIn(0f, 1f)
75:             drawRect(
76:                 color = b.color.copy(alpha = fade),
77:                 topLeft = Offset(x, y),
78:                 size = Size(b.size, b.size * (0.6f + 0.4f * sin(b.spin * t * 6f)))
79:             )
80:         }
81:     }
82: }
83: 
84: private const val DURATION = 1.8f
85:

1: ---
2: title: "Place-to-Orbit Sandbox Controls — Design Spec"
3: project: SophiaSpace
4: date: 2026-06-10
5: status: Approved (in-chat, 2026-06-10); implemented same day
6: ---
7: 
8: # Place-to-Orbit Sandbox Controls
9: 
10: ## Problem
11: 
12: The Phase-1 sandbox handed the kid's fling velocity straight back to the
13: simulation on release. For a 5-year-old's motor skills the result felt random:
14: tiny gesture differences produced wildly different orbits, and most drags ended
15: in "it flew away" — a fail-state. Tom's intent was *"move a planet somewhere
16: new and watch how the orbit changes."* The pedagogy research brief
17: (docs/Teaching the Solar System…) independently backs the change: congruent
18: drag gestures, zero fail-states, observe-before-explain.
19: 
20: ## Behavior
21: 
22: - **Grab** — only bodies in the scenario's `draggableIds` respond. Sun never
23:   moves; in Earth–Moon only the Moon is grabbable (Earth stays home).
24: - **While dragging** — the body follows her finger. Two live previews draw on
25:   the canvas: a dashed **orbit ring** around the attractor at the clamped
26:   current radius, and a **ghost planet** showing where the body will settle.
27: - **On release** — the fling is ignored entirely. The body's distance is
28:   clamped to `[minDropRadius, maxDropRadius]`, and it is placed on a clean
29:   **counterclockwise circular orbit** at that distance:
30:   `v = sqrt(GM_UNIT · (attractor.mass + body.mass) / r)` relative to the
31:   attractor. In the true-2-body Earth–M
<truncated 26 bytes>
enter
32:   momentum is re-zeroed so the scene never drifts off screen.
33: - **Nothing escapes, crashes, or gets lost.** The clamps + circular release
34:   make ESCAPED / CRASHED / LOST / WOBBLY unreachable by construction; the
35:   ConsequenceDetector and its tests stay intact for future modules.
36: - **Kept:** TOO_HOT / TOO_COLD (now purely positional) with the continuous
37:   temperature tint, RESTORED + sticker, and Put It Back.
38: 
39: ## Narration changes (Scripts.kt)
40: 
41: - TOO_HOT / TOO_COLD reframed to habitable-zone, permanent-home language
42:   ("If Earth **lived** this close… it would **always** be too hot") — never
43:   weather or season words. Firewalls the "closer = summer" lifelong
44:   misconception (research pitfall #2).
45: - New `SANDBOX_NEW_ORBIT` line, spoken once per playground the first time she
46:   settles a body ≥25 % away from its home radius: "Closer to the middle means
47:   faster — farther means slower!" (the honest Kepler takeaway).
48: 
49: ## Tunables
50: 
51: | Scenario | minDropRadius | maxDropRadius | draggable |
52: |---|---|---|---|
53: | Sun's family | 0.30 AU | 2.2 AU | Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars |
54: | Earth–Moon | 0.30 | 1.5 | Moon only |
55: 
56: ## Known, accepted trades
57: 
58: - Every settled orbit is a perfect circle — leans slightly into the "orbits
59:   are perfect circles" misconception, accepted as scope-not-direction
60:   simplification for age 5.
61: - Fling-to-escape (a PRD centerpiece moment) is removed. GATE 1 now watches
62:   for "can it fly away?" as the signal to revisit a deliberate-escape gesture.
63: 
64: ## Tests
65: 
66: `PlacementTest.kt` (pure JVM): settle at any radius → bound (energy < 0),
67: near-circular (e ≈ 0), exact clamped distance, counterclockwise; dead-center
68: drop is safe; orbit stays circular over a full integrated period; Earth–Moon
69: barycenter momentum ≈ 0 after settle. Existing 18 tests stay green.
70:

1: # Sophia's Space App Audit Report
2: 
3: This document contains a comprehensive architectural and code quality audit of **Sophia's Space** (v1.3.1, build 5), a fully narrated solar-system educational playground application designed for pre-readers (specifically 5-year-olds).
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## 📋 Executive Summary
8: 
9: Sophia's Space is built on modern Android principles with a solid foundation in Jetpack Compose, Kotlin 2.3.20, and Jetpack Compose Navigation 3. It utilizes a symplectic physics integrator and accurate astronomical math, providing high-fidelity pedagogical demonstrations of orbital mechanics, day/night cycles, and moon phases without overwhelming its target audience.
10: 
11: However, the audit revealed a **critical resource leak and performance issue** in [SoundSynth.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/SoundSynth.kt) related to the manual spawning of raw OS threads and `AudioTrack` objects for playing short sound effects. Fixing this issue will prevent potential device-specific crashes and lockups.
12: 
13: ---
14: 
15: ## 🛠️ Architecture & Tech Stack
16: 
17: - **Core Framework**: Kotlin 2.3.20 & Jetpack Compose (BOM `2026.03.01`)
18: - **Target SDK**: 36 (Android 16 preview/latest); **Min SDK**: 24 (Android 7.0)
19: - **Navigation**: Jetpack Navigation 3 (`androidx.navigation3:navigation3-runtime:1.0.1`) — a modern declarative navigation API.
20: - **Persistence**: Jetpack Preferences DataStore (`androidx.data
<truncated 4550 bytes>
aspace/physics/GravitySim.kt) is exceptionally well-coded:
91: - **Symplectic Euler**: Updating velocity first (`v += a * dt`) and then position using the new velocity (`x += v * dt`) is a symplectic scheme. This keeps planetary orbits stable indefinitely, preventing the spiraling drift associated with standard Forward Euler.
92: - **Softening Length**: Using `softening = 0.05` inside the denominator `(d2 + eps2)^1.5` prevents singularities (division by zero) when the child drags a planet directly on top of the Sun.
93: - **Substep Clamping**: Clamping simulation steps to `maxSubStepsPerAdvance = 8` protects against "death spirals" (where a frames-per-second drop causes the engine to run more and more physics frames, compounding the lag).
94: 
95: ### 🟢 Keplerian Ephemeris Solver
96: The Kepler solver in [Ephemeris.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/sky/Ephemeris.kt#L73-L76) solves $M = E - e \sin E$ using Newton-Raphson:
97: 
98: ```kotlin
99: var eAnom = m + e * sin(m)
100: repeat(8) { eAnom -= (eAnom - e * sin(eAnom) - m) / (1 - e * cos(eAnom)) }
101: ```
102: 
103: A fixed limit of 8 iterations is extremely stable and converges to high-precision solutions for low-eccentricity solar system bodies (all planets have $e < 0.21$).
104: 
105: ---
106: 
107: ## 🌐 Build Server / Landing Page Audit
108: 
109: The landing page [index.html](file:///y:/ReynoldsFamily/builds/index.html) in the secondary workspace provides downloads for family builds, including `SophiasSpace-1.3.1.apk`.
110: - **UX Excellence**: When intercepting APK/EXE downloads, it prompts for subscription but includes a prominent bypass ("No thanks, just download") and handles fetch failures gracefully.
111: - **CORS Handling**: The endpoint posts directly to ConvertKit API and handles network drops by executing the download programmatically, ensuring users are never blocked.
112:

1: package com.example.sophiaspace.sky
2: 
3: import org.junit.Assert.assertEquals
4: import org.junit.Assert.assertNull
5: import org.junit.Assert.assertTrue
6: import org.junit.Test
7: 
8: class AstroMathTest {
9: 
10:     // 2026-06-10 00:00:00 UTC in epoch millis.
11:     private val nowMillis = 1781049600000L
12: 
13:     @Test
14:     fun `julian day of the unix epoch is 2440587_5`() {
15:         assertEquals(2440587.5, AstroMath.julianDay(0L), 1e-9)
16:     }
17: 
18:     @Test
19:     fun `gmst is in 0-360 and gains a sidereal degree per day`() {
20:         val jd = AstroMath.julianDay(nowMillis)
21:         val g0 = AstroMath.gmstDeg(jd)
22:         val g1 = AstroMath.gmstDeg(jd + 1.0)
23:         assertTrue(g0 in 0.0..360.0)
24:         val advance = ((g1 - g0) % 360.0 + 360.0) % 360.0
25:         assertEquals(0.9856, advance, 0.01) // sidereal gain ≈ 0.9856°/day
26:     }
27: 
28:     @Test
29:     fun `polaris altitude is about the observer latitude, azimuth about north`() {
30:         // Polaris: RA 37.95°, Dec +89.26°. Observer at Denver-ish (39.7N, -105.0E).
31:         val jd = AstroMath.julianDay(nowMillis)
32:         val (alt, az) = AstroMath.altAz(37.95, 89.26, 39.7, -105.0, jd)
33:         assertEquals(39.7, alt, 1.5)
34:         assertTrue("azimuth should be near north: $az", az < 3.0 || az > 357.0)
35:     }
36: 
37:     @Test
38:     fun `a star on the celestial equator culminates at 90 minus latitude`() {
39:         var maxAlt = -90.0
40:         val jd0 = AstroMath.julianDay(nowMillis)
41:         for (m in 0 until 1440) {
42:             val (alt, _) = AstroMath.altAz(180.0, 0.0, 40.0, 0.0, jd0 + m / 1440.0)
43:             if (alt > maxAlt) maxAlt = alt
44:         }
45:         assertEquals(50.0, maxAlt, 0.5)
46:     }
47: 
48:     @Test
49:     fun `projection - the look-center lands at the origin and behind-camera culls`() {
50:         val center = AstroMath.project(45.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)
51:         assertEquals(0.0, center!!.first, 1e-9)
52:         assertEquals(0.0, center.second, 1e-9)
53:         assertNull(AstroMath.project(-45.0, 0.0, 45.0, 180.0))
54:     }
55: 
56:     @Test
57:     fun `projection - a star above the look-center projects upward`() {
58:         val p = AstroMath.project(55.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)!!
59:         assertTrue("higher star should have +y: ${p.second}", p.second > 0.0)
60:         assertEquals(0.0, p.first, 1e-6)
61:     }
62: 
63:     @Test
64:     fun `camera projection - center at origin, offsets are exact tangents, behind culls`() {
65:         val center = AstroMath.projectCamera(45.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)!!
66:         assertEquals(0.0, center.first, 1e-9)
67:         assertEquals(0.0, center.second, 1e-9)
68:         // 10° above the look-center must land at exactly tan(10°) —
69:         // that's what makes the overlay pan at the camera image's rate.
70:         val above = AstroMath.projectCamera(55.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)!!
71:         assertEquals(Math.tan(Math.toRadians(10.0)), above.second, 1e-6)
72:         assertEquals(0.0, above.first, 1e-6)
73:         assertNull(AstroMath.projectCamera(-45.0, 0.0, 45.0, 180.0))
74:     }
75: }

9: 
10: /**
11:  * Place-to-Orbit release: wherever a body is dropped, [Scenario.settleIntoOrbit]
12:  * must produce a bound, near-circular, counterclockwise orbit at the clamped
13:  * radius — regardless of how wild the incoming "fling" velocity was.
14:  */
15: class PlacementTest {
16: 
17:     @Test
18:     fun `dropped earth settles into a bound circular orbit at any radius`() {
19:         for (r in listOf(0.35, 0.7, 1.0, 1.6, 2.2)) {
20:             val scenario = Scenarios.sunFamily()
21:             val earth = scenario.watched()
22:             // Arbitrary angle at distance r, with garbage fling velocity that
23:             // must be ignored entirely.
24:             earth.x = r * 0.6
25:             earth.y = r * 0.8
26:             earth.vx = 37.0
27:             earth.vy = -12.0
28:             scenario.settleIntoOrbit(earth)
29: 
30:             val sun = scenario.attractor()
31:             assertEquals("settled at wrong radius", r, earth.distanceTo(sun), 1e-9)
32:             assertTrue("orbit not bound at r=$r", OrbitalMath.specificEnergy(earth, sun) < 0.0)
33:             val ecc = OrbitalMath.eccentricity(earth, sun)
34:             assertTrue("orbit not circular at r=$r: e=$ecc", ecc < 0.01)
35:         }
36:     }
37: 
38:     @Test
39:     fun `dr
<truncated 2586 bytes>
 settles around earth with a stationary barycenter`() {
102:         val scenario = Scenarios.earthMoon()
103:         val sim = scenario.createSim()
104:         val moon = scenario.watched()
105:         val earth = scenario.attractor()
106:         moon.x = 1.3; moon.y = 0.4
107:         moon.vx = -50.0; moon.vy = 50.0 // wild fling, ignored
108:         scenario.settleIntoOrbit(moon)
109: 
110:         // Pair momentum re-zeroed so the scene never drifts off screen.
111:         val px = earth.mass * earth.vx + moon.mass * moon.vx
112:         val py = earth.mass * earth.vy + moon.mass * moon.vy
113:         assertEquals(0.0, hypot(px, py), 1e-9)
114: 
115:         assertTrue(OrbitalMath.specificEnergy(moon, earth) < 0.0)
116:         // Eccentricity measured with earth-only mu reads ~m/M for the true
117:         // 2-body pair; anything under 0.05 is visually a clean circle.
118:         assertTrue(OrbitalMath.eccentricity(moon, earth) < 0.05)
119: 
120:         // Separation must hold through a full relative-orbit period.
121:         val r0 = moon.distanceTo(earth)
122:         val a = r0
123:         val period = a.pow(1.5) / kotlin.math.sqrt(1.0 + moon.mass)
124:         var t = 0.0
125:         var maxDeviation = 0.0
126:         while (t < period) {
127:             sim.step(0.001)
128:             t += 0.001
129:             maxDeviation = maxOf(maxDeviation, abs(moon.distanceTo(earth) - r0) / r0)
130:         }
131:         assertTrue("separation drifted ${maxDeviation * 100}%", maxDeviation < 0.03)
132:     }
133: 
134:     @Test
135:     fun `settling clears kinematic and lost flags`() {
136:         val scenario = Scenarios.earthMoon()
137:         val moon = scenario.watched()
138:         moon.kinematic = true
139:         moon.lostInSpace = true
140:         moon.x = 0.8; moon.y = 0.0
141:         scenario.settleIntoOrbit(moon)
142:         assertTrue(!moon.kinematic && !moon.lostInSpace)
143:     }
144: }

  "CodeContent": "\"# Sophia's Space Codebase Outline & Audit\\n\\nThis document outlines and audits every file, package, and component of the **Sophia's Space** codebase. Each part of the system has been checked against the product requirements, pedagogical intent, and design plans.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. Architectural Architecture\\n\\nSophia's Space is structured into clean, decoupled layers, ensuring the core physics, math, and data logic remain independent of Android frameworks for easy JVM unit testing.\\n\\n```mermaid\\ngraph TD\\n    UI[ui/ screens & components] --> NAV[Navigation.kt & NavigationKeys.kt]\\n    UI --> PHYS_PLAY[physics/ Scenarios & CatchMars]\\n    UI --> SKY_MATH[sky/ AstroMath & Ephemeris]\\n    UI --> AUDIO[audio/ NarrationManager & SoundSynth]\\n    UI --> DATA[data/ AppSettings]\\n    PHYS_PLAY --> INTEGRATOR[physics/ GravitySim & Body]\\n    SKY_MATH --> MATH_LIBS[sky/ AstroMath]\\n    AUDIO --> SCRIPTS[audio/ Scripts]\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. File-by-File Outline & Audit\\n\\n### Root Package (`com.example.sophiaspace`)\\n\\n#### \ud83d\udcc4 [MainActivity.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/MainActivity.kt)\\n- **Role**: Entry point of the app; initializes global singletons (`AppSettings`, `NarrationManager`, `SoundSynth`) and keeps them in sync with UI settings via flow collection.\\n- **Wording / Wires**: Automatically ducks sound effect volumes when the narrator speaks (`volumeScale = 0.35f`).\\n- **Audit**: Lifecycle-safe. Uses `lifecycle.repeatOnLifecycle(Lifecycle.State.STARTED)` so flow collection suspends when backgrounded, preventing background battery drain.\\n\\n#### \ud83d\udcc4 [Navigation.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/Navigation.kt)\\n- **Role**: Defines the application nav graph using Navigation 3's declarative `NavDisplay` and `entryProvider`.\\n- **Audit**: Clean structure, passes parameters down to screen composables without dependency injection frameworks.\\n\\n###\n<truncated 15400 bytes>",
  "Description": "\"Written the comprehensive file-by-file audit and outline of Sophia's Space to C:\\\\Users\\\\thoma\\\\.gemini\\\\antigravity\\\\brain\\\\519c5a72-c69e-4845-a55c-8f0d04101e15\\\\sophias_space_code_outline.md.\"",

By migrating from DisposableEffect to LifecycleResumeEffect, the rotation sensor listener is correctly unregistered when the app goes into the background, preventing significant battery drain.\",\"UserFacing\":true}",
  "CodeContent": "\"# Implementation Plan: Fix Background Sensor Resource Leak\\n\\nAddress background battery drain/sensor leak inside the Phase-0 diagnostic screen (`SensorSpikeScreen`).\\n\\n## User Review Required\\n\\n> [!NOTE]\\n> This change is minor and has no user-visible UI impact, but it preserves battery life when the app goes to the background while viewing the developer tools/diagnostics screen.\\n\\n## Open Questions\\n\\nNone.\\n\\n## Proposed Changes\\n\\n### UI Components\\n\\n#### [MODIFY] [SensorSpikeScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/spike/SensorSpikeScreen.kt)\\nMigrate the `OrientationProvider` registration from `DisposableEffect` to `LifecycleResumeEffect` so that the sensor is unregistered when the Activity is paused (backgrounded) and re-registered when resumed, matching the `SkyViewScreen` lifecycle behavior.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Verification Plan\\n\\n### Automated Tests\\nRun unit tests to ensure no regressions are introduced:\\n- `.\\\\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest`\\n\\n### Manual Verification\\n1. Open the **Grown-up Corner** (2-second hold gate).\\n2. Open **Open sensor spike \ud83e\udded**.\\n3. Verify the sensor is actively reading and the needle rotates.\\n4. Press home/minimize the app.\\n5. Verify (via Logcat or CPU profiler) that no sensor events are processed in the background.\\n6. Re-open the app and verify sensor tracking resumes immediately.\\n\"",
  "Description": "\"Created implementation_plan.md in the artifacts directory.\"",
  "Overwrite": "true",

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: import asyncio
2: import sys
3: 
4: async def try_connect(ip, port):
5:     try:
6:         conn = asyncio.open_connection(ip, port)
7:         reader, writer = await asyncio.wait_for(conn, timeout=0.15)
8:         writer.close()
9:         await writer.wait_closed()
10:         print(f"FOUND:{port}", flush=True)
11:         return port
12:     except Exception:
13:         return None
14: 
15: async def main():
16:     ip = "192.168.50.67"
17:     start_port = 35000
18:     end_port = 45000
19:     
20:     print(f"Scanning {ip} from {start_port} to {end_port}...")
21:     tasks = []
22:     # Scan in batches to avoid OS resource limits
23:     batch_size = 500
24:     for i in range(start_port, end_port, batch_size):
25:         batch_tasks = [try_connect(ip, port) for port in range(i, min(i + batch_size, end_port))]
26:         results = await asyncio.gather(*batch_tasks)
27:         found = [r for r in results if r is not None]
28:         if found:
29:             print(f"Scan complete. Active port: {found[0]}")
30:             sys.exit(0)
31:             
32:     print("No open ports found.")
33: 
34: if __name__ == "__main__":
35:     asyncio.run(main())
36:

1: ---
2: name: android-cli
3: description: Orchestrates Android development tasks including project creation, deployment, SDK management, and environment diagnostics using the `android` command-line tool.
4: ---
5: # Android CLI Specialist
6: 
7: This skill provides instructions for using the `android` CLI tool. The tool includes various commands for creating projects, running applications, interacting with devices, and managing the CLI environment.
8: 
9: ## Installation
10: 
11: If the android tool is not in the path install it. To install run the following command:
12: Linux: curl -fsSL https://dl.google.com/android/cli/latest/linux_x86_64/install.sh | bash
13: Mac: curl -fsSL https://dl.google.com/android/cli/latest/darwin_arm64/install.sh | bash
14: Windows: curl.exe -fsSL https://dl.google.com/android/cli/latest/windows_x86_64/install.cmd -o "%TEMP%\i.cmd" && "%TEMP%\i.cmd"
15: 
16: ## SDK management
17: To manage the installation of Android SDKs and tools, use the `sdk` command. For example:
18: 
19: - `android sdk install <package>[@<version>]...`: Install specific packages. Multiple packages can be specified, separated by spaces. `<version>` defaults to latest. For example: `android sdk install platforms/android-30@2 platforms/android-34`
20: - `android sdk update [<pkg-name>]`: Update a specific package or all packages to the latest version.
21: - `android sdk remove <pkg-name>`: Remove a package from the local SDK.
22: - `android sdk list --all`: List installed and available SDK packages.
23: 
24: ## Project creation
25: Create projects from templa

  "CodeContent": "\"# Walkthrough: Diagnostic Screen Sensor Lifecycle Fix\\n\\nThis document details the diagnostic screen bug fix, validation results, and deployment.\\n\\n## \ud83d\udee0\ufe0f Changes Implemented\\n\\n### Sensor Spike Diagnostic Screen\\n- **Modified**: [SensorSpikeScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/spike/SensorSpikeScreen.kt)\\n- **Fix**: Replaced the `DisposableEffect(Unit)` sensor registration block with Compose's lifecycle-aware `LifecycleResumeEffect(Unit)`.\\n  - Prior to this change, backgrounding the app while the sensor spike screen was open would keep the orientation sensor listener registered, leading to rapid battery depletion.\\n  - With this fix, the sensor listener automatically unregisters when the activity is paused (backgrounded) and re-registers when it returns to the foreground.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83e\uddea Validation & Verification Results\\n\\n### 1. Automated Tests\\nRan the full JUnit project test suite to verify no regression of orbits, day/night tracker, moon phase geometry, and rocket flight simulation.\\n- **Command**: `.\\\\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest`\\n- **Result**: `BUILD SUCCESSFUL` with all 55/55 unit tests green.\\n\\n### 2. Manual Verification & On-Device Run\\n- **Build**: Compiled `SophiasSpace-1.4.1` debug build.\\n- **Port Scan**: Detected dynamic wireless ADB port **44297** on device `192.168.50.67`.\\n- **Deployment**: Successfully pushed and installed the updated APK to the device via ADB:\\n  ```powershell\\n  adb -s 192.168.50.67:44297 install -r app-debug.apk\\n  ```\\n- **Device checks**:\\n  - The **Sensor Spike** diagnostics screen reads orientation data correctly when open.\\n  - When the app is minimized, the sensor stops processing background events.\\n  - Re-entering the app resumes sensor tracking immediately.\\n\"",

## Open Questions

None blocking. Items 5–6 are optional polish — Tom can veto.

## Status of the Original Plan

### 1. [MODIFY] [OrientationProvider.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/sky/OrientationProvider.kt) + [SkyViewScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt) — calibration prompt never fires (PRD-intent gap)

 pass; add a small JUnit for the nearest-planet selection if it's extracted into a pure helper (preferred).

### Manual (on the Pixel, wireless adb available at 192.168.50.67:38001 — port may rotate)
1. Item 1: in Sky View magic-window mode, wave the phone near a magnet/metal until accuracy drops — the lazy-8 line should play once, and not again within a minute.
2. Item 2: bump `SYNTH_VERSION`, reinstall, confirm sounds still play (new files render) and old `synth_*.wav` are gone from cache.
3. Item 3: tonight Venus and Jupiter are both up — point between them; the ring should claim the closer one.
4. Item 7: dashboard renders, Phase 0 shows the new ticks, no console errors.

## 🔒 Project guardrails (non-negotiable — from the PRD + research brief)
- **Pedagogy:** never an Earth-shadow/creeping-disc moon visual; the Sun never "moves/sets/sleeps" (we spin); too-hot/too-cold stays in habitable-zone "lived/always" language — never weather/season words; no fail-states (misses are gags).
- **Privacy:** CAMERA stays the only permission; preview-only, never stored/sent; no internet/location/accounts.
- **Release checklist for ANY shipped change:** bump `versionCode` + `versionName` in `app/build.gradle.kts` → `gradlew testDebugUnitTest assembleDebug` → copy APK to `Y:\ReynoldsFamily\builds\SophiasSpace-<version>.apk` (never overwrite a published version) → update the site card's version chip + download href in `Y:\ReynoldsFamily\builds\index.html` → append a dated entry to the dashboard `CHANGES` array → `adb install -r` to the Pixel. Next free slot: **v1.4.2 (build 10)** — note your already-applied SensorSpike fix rides in this release.
- **Site caveat:** `Y:\...\index.html` is sometimes regenerated by an external site build — re-read before editing, and know that the download-popup bypass fix (`programmaticDownload` flag) and the Sophia's Space card must survive any regeneration.

1: # Implementation Plan: Fix Background Sensor Resource Leak
2: 
3: Address background battery drain/sensor leak inside the Phase-0 diagnostic screen (`SensorSpikeScreen`).
4: 
5: ## User Review Required
6: 
7: > [!NOTE]
8: > This change is minor and has no user-visible UI impact, but it preserves battery life when the app goes to the background while viewing the developer tools/diagnostics screen.
9: 
10: ## Open Questions
11: 
12: None.
13: 
14: ## Proposed Changes
15: 
16: ### UI Components
17: 
18: #### [MODIFY] [SensorSpikeScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/spike/SensorSpikeScreen.kt)
19: Migrate the `OrientationProvider` registration from `DisposableEffect` to `LifecycleResumeEffect` so that the sensor is unregistered when the Activity is paused (backgrounded) and re-registered when resumed, matching the `SkyViewScreen` lifecycle behavior.
20: 
21: ---
22: 
23: ## Verification Plan
24: 
25: ### Automated Tests
26: Run unit tests to ensure no regressions are introduced:
27: - `.\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest`
28: 
29: ### Manual Verification
30: 1. Open the **Grown-up Corner** (2-second hold gate).
31: 2. Open **Open sensor spike 🧭**.
32: 3. Verify the sensor is actively reading and the needle rotates.
33: 4. Press home/minimize the app.
34: 5. Verify (via Logcat or CPU profiler) that no sensor events are processed in the background.
35: 6. Re-open the app and verify sensor tracking resumes immediately.
36:

1: package com.example.sophiaspace.sky
2: 
3: import android.content.Context
4: import android.hardware.Sensor
5: import android.hardware.SensorEvent
6: import android.hardware.SensorEventListener
7: import android.hardware.SensorManager
8: import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.MutableStateFlow
9: import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.StateFlow
10: import kotlin.math.asin
11: import kotlin.math.atan2
12: import kotlin.math.sqrt
13: 
14: /** Where the back of the phone is pointing, in sky coordinates. */
15: data class Pointing(
16:     /** Degrees clockwise from magnetic north, 0..360. */
17:     val azimuthDeg: Float,
18:     /** Degrees above the horizon, -90..90. */
19:     val altitudeDeg: Float
20: )
21: 
22: /**
23:  * Rotation-vector sensor → "magic window" look direction.
24:  *
25:  * The look vector is the device's -Z axis (out the back of the phone) expressed
26:  * in world ENU coordinates: hold the phone up like a window and the reported
27:  * azimuth/altitude is the patch of sky behind it. The raw vector is smoothed
28:  * with an exponential moving average and re-normalized — smoothing Euler angles
29:  * directly causes the classic 359°→0° snap.
30:  *
31:  * Register in onResume / unregister in onPause; the sensor must never run
32:  * while the screen is off (battery).
33:  */
34: class OrientationProvider(context: Context) : SensorEventListener {
35: 
36:     private val sensorManager = context.getSystemService(Context.SENSOR_SERVICE) as SensorManager
37:     private val rotationSensor
<truncated 1089 bytes>
5: 
66:     fun stop() {
67:         sensorManager.unregisterListener(this)
68:         primed = false
69:         _pointing.value = null
70:     }
71: 
72:     override fun onSensorChanged(event: SensorEvent) {
73:         if (event.sensor.type != Sensor.TYPE_ROTATION_VECTOR) return
74:         SensorManager.getRotationMatrixFromVector(rotationMatrix, event.values)
75: 
76:         // World-frame direction of the device -Z axis (out the back of the phone).
77:         // getRotationMatrixFromVector returns R with world = R * device, row-major.
78:         val lookX = -rotationMatrix[2]
79:         val lookY = -rotationMatrix[5]
80:         val lookZ = -rotationMatrix[8]
81: 
82:         if (!primed) {
83:             smoothX = lookX; smoothY = lookY; smoothZ = lookZ
84:             primed = true
85:         } else {
86:             val k = smoothing
87:             smoothX += k * (lookX - smoothX)
88:             smoothY += k * (lookY - smoothY)
89:             smoothZ += k * (lookZ - smoothZ)
90:         }
91:         val len = sqrt(smoothX * smoothX + smoothY * smoothY + smoothZ * smoothZ)
92:         if (len < 1e-6f) return
93:         val nx = smoothX / len
94:         val ny = smoothY / len
95:         val nz = smoothZ / len
96: 
97:         // World frame: X = east, Y = magnetic north, Z = up.
98:         val azimuth = Math.toDegrees(atan2(nx.toDouble(), ny.toDouble())).toFloat().let {
99:             if (it < 0f) it + 360f else it
100:         }
101:         val altitude = Math.toDegrees(asin(nz.toDouble().coerceIn(-1.0, 1.0))).toFloat()
102: 
103:         _pointing.value = Pointing(azimuth, altitude)
104:     }
105: 
106:     override fun onAccuracyChanged(sensor: Sensor?, accuracy: Int) = Unit
107: 
108:     companion object {
109:         const val DEFAULT_SMOOTHING = 0.18f
110:         /** Snappier tracking for camera AR — the overlay must keep up. */
111:         const val AR_SMOOTHING = 0.45f
112:     }
113: }

6:  * because the listener is five.

8: enum class ScriptKey(val line: String) {
9:     // Home
10:     HOME_WELCOME("Welcome to space, Sophia! Tap somewhere to explore!"),
11:     HOME_SANDBOX("The playground! You can move the planets yourself!"),
12:     HOME_COMING_SOON("We're still building this one. Soon, I promise!"),
13:     HOME_ORBITS("Spinning! Watch everybody circle the Sun, around and around!"),
14:     HOME_DAY_NIGHT("Day and night! Give the Earth a spin with your finger!"),
15:     HOME_MOON("The Moon! Let's see why it looks different every night."),
16: 
17:     // Orbits lesson
18:     ORBITS_INTRO("Everybody is going around the Sun — around and around, like a big dance! Slide the magic clock to make time go faster."),
19:     ORBITS_YEAR_DONE("Look! Earth went all the way around the Sun. That's one whole year — one more birthday for you!"),
20:     ORBITS_SPIN_MARKER("See the little dot on Earth? Earth is spinning like a top WHILE it goes around. Spinning makes days. Going around makes years!"),
21:     ORBITS_FAST("Wheee, time is zooming! Watch how the close planets zip around fastest."),
22: 
23:     // Day & Night lesson — "WE spin", the Sun never moves or sleeps.
24:     DAYNIGHT_INTRO("Here's our Earth, and here's our little house. Put your finger on Earth and giv
<truncated 2536 bytes>
t there in your real sky!"),
50:     SKY_MOON_TAP("That's our Moon — looking exactly like it does outside tonight! Go check!"),
51: 
52:     // Sandbox — framing
53:     SANDBOX_EARTH_MOON_INTRO("Let's zoom way, way in! Here's our Earth, and our Moon going around it. Try dragging the Moon with your finger!"),
54:     SANDBOX_SUN_INTRO("Here's the Sun and its family! Everybody is circling the Sun. Try dragging a planet and see what happens!"),
55:     SANDBOX_GRABBED("Whee! You've got it! Where will you put it?"),
56:     SANDBOX_NEW_ORBIT("Look at that! A whole new path. Closer to the middle means faster — farther means slower!"),
57: 
58:     // Sandbox — consequences
59:     CONSEQUENCE_ESCAPED("Oh no! It's flying away into space! Gravity can't hold it anymore. Can you put it back?"),
60:     CONSEQUENCE_CRASHED("Sizzle! Too close! It fell right in. Let's put it back where it belongs."),
61:     CONSEQUENCE_LOST("It's so far away now, it's lost in space! Press the big button to bring it home."),
62:     // Habitable-zone framing on purpose: "lived / always", never weather or

14: /**
15:  * Tiny 8-bit-style synthesizer for interaction juice (SophiaQuest lineage).
16:  *
17:  * The six effects are rendered to small WAV files in the cache dir once, then
18:  * played through a single shared [SoundPool] — which owns the thread/voice
19:  * pool, enforces a concurrency cap, and applies volume live. (The previous
20:  * thread-per-sound AudioTrack scheme could exhaust the device's AudioTrack
21:  * limit under a five-year-old's tap rate, and leaked tracks on error paths.)
22:  *
23:  * The NarrationManager ducks this while speaking via [volumeScale].
24:  */
25: object SoundSynth {
26:     private const val SAMPLE_RATE = 22050
27: 
28:     @Volatile
29:     var volumeScale: Float = 1.0f
30: 
31:     private enum class Effect { POP, WHOOSH, SIZZLE, DRIFT, CHIME, FANFARE }
32: 
33:     @Volatile
34:     private var pool: SoundPool? = null
35:     private val soundIds = ConcurrentHashMap<Effect, Int>()
36:     private val readySamples = ConcurrentHashMap<Int, Boolean>()
37: 
38:     /** Call once from MainActivity.onCreate. Idempotent; renders off-thread. */
39:     fun init(context: Context) {
40:
<truncated 5644 bytes>
t) * 12000.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
176:         }
177:         return buffer
178:     }
179: 
180:     private fun renderFanfare(): ShortArray {
181:         val numSamples = samples(600)
182:         val buffer = ShortArray(numSamples)
183:         for (i in 0 until numSamples) {
184:             val t = i.toDouble() / SAMPLE_RATE
185:             val noteIdx = i / (numSamples / 4)
186:             val freq = when (noteIdx) {
187:                 0 -> 523.25
188:                 1 -> 783.99
189:                 2 -> 659.25
190:                 else -> 1046.50
191:             }
192:             val vibrato = if (noteIdx == 3) sin(2.0 * PI * 12.0 * t) * 8.0 else 0.0
193:             val envelope = 1.0 - (i.toDouble() / numSamples)
194:             buffer[i] = (sin(2.0 * PI * (freq + vibrato) * t) * 12000.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
195:         }
196:         return buffer
197:     }
198: 
199:     private fun samples(durationMs: Int) = (SAMPLE_RATE * (durationMs / 1000.0)).toInt()
200: 
201:     /** Minimal 16-bit mono PCM WAV writer. */
202:     private fun writeWav(file: File, pcm: ShortArray) {
203:         DataOutputStream(BufferedOutputStream(FileOutputStream(file))).use { o ->
204:             fun le32(v: Int) {
205:                 o.write(v and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 8) and 0xFF)
206:                 o.write((v shr 16) and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 24) and 0xFF)
207:             }
208:             fun le16(v: Int) {
209:                 o.write(v and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 8) and 0xFF)
210:             }
211:             val dataSize = pcm.size * 2
212:             o.writeBytes("RIFF"); le32(36 + dataSize); o.writeBytes("WAVE")
213:             o.writeBytes("fmt "); le32(16); le16(1); le16(1)
214:             le32(SAMPLE_RATE); le32(SAMPLE_RATE * 2); le16(2); le16(16)
215:             o.writeBytes("data"); le32(dataSize)
216:             for (s in pcm) le16(s.toInt() and 0xFFFF)
217:         }
218:     }
219: }

1: package com.example.sophiaspace.ui.components
2: 
3: import androidx.compose.foundation.Canvas
4: import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
5: import androidx.compose.runtime.LaunchedEffect
6: import androidx.compose.runtime.getValue
7: import androidx.compose.runtime.mutableFloatStateOf
8: import androidx.compose.runtime.remember
9: import androidx.compose.runtime.setValue
10: import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
11: import androidx.compose.ui.geometry.Offset
12: import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color
13: import kotlin.math.sin
14: import kotlin.random.Random
15: 
16: private class TwinkleStar(val fx: Float, val fy: Float, val size: Float, val phase: Float, val speed: Float)
17: 
18: /**
19:  * Gently twinkling star backdrop drawn behind every screen.
20:  * Stars are generated once per composition; only their alpha animates.
21:  */
22: @Composable
23: fun SpaceBackground(modifier: Modifier = Modifier, starCount: Int = 90) {
24:     val stars = remember {
25:         val rng = Random(42)
26:         List(starCount) {
27:             TwinkleStar(
28:                 fx = rng.nextFloat(),
29:                 fy = rng.nextFloat(),
30:                 size = 1.2f + rng.nextFloat() * 2.2f,
31:                 phase = rng.nextFloat() * 6.2832f,
32:                 speed = 0.4f + rng.nextFloat() * 0.9f
33:             )
34:         }
35:     }
36: 
37:     var time by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(0f) }
38:     LaunchedEffect(Unit) {
39:         val start = System.nanoTime()
40:         while (true) {
41:             androidx.compose.runtime.withFrameNanos { now ->
42:                 time = (now - start) / 1_000_000_000f
43:             }
44:         }
45:     }
46: 
47:     Canvas(modifier = modifier) {
48:         for (s in stars) {
49:             val alpha = 0.25f + 0.55f * (0.5f + 0.5f * sin(time * s.speed + s.phase))
50:             drawCircle(
51:                 color = Color.White.copy(alpha = alpha),
52:                 radius = s.size,
53:                 center = Offset(s.fx * size.width, s.fy * size.height)
54:             )
55:         }
56:     }
57: }
58:

485: function renderTables(){
486:   $('#decisionBody').innerHTML = DECISIONS.map(r=>'<tr><td><strong>'+esc(r[0])+'</strong></td><td>'+esc(r[1])+'</td><td>'+esc(r[2])+'</td></tr>').join('');

490: function renderChanges(){
491:   const all = (state.notes||[]).concat(CHANGES);
492:   $('#changeList').innerHTML = all.map(c=>'<div class="change"><div class="date">'+esc(c.date)+'</div><div class="ct">'+esc(c.title)+'</div><div class="cb">'+esc(c.body)+'</div></div>').join('');
493: }
494: 
495: /* ---------------- EVENTS ---------------- */
496: $('#noteForm').addEventListener('submit', e=>{
497:   e.preventDefault();
498:   const v=$('#noteText').value.trim(); if(!v) return;
499:   const d = new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10);
500:   state.notes = state.notes||[]; state.notes.unshift({date:d, title:'Local note', body:v});
501:   $('#noteText').value=''; save(); renderChanges();
502: });
503: $('#resetBtn').addEventListener('click', ()=>{
504:   if(!confirm('Reset all task ticks and gate marks back to the planned baseline?')) return;
505:   state.taskDone={}; state.gates={};
506:   PHASES.forEach((p,pi)=>p.milestones.forEach((m,mi)=>m.tasks.forEach((t,ti)=>{ state.taskDone[tkey(pi,mi,ti)]=!!t.d; })));
507:   save(); renderAll();
508: });

510:   const blob=new Blob([JSON.stringify(state,null,2)],{type:'application/json'});
511:   const a=document.createElement('a'); a.href=URL.createObjectURL(blob); a.download='sophiaspace-dashboard-state.json'; a.click(); URL.revokeObjectURL(a.href);
512: });
513: $('#importInput').addEventListener('change', e=>{
514:   const f=e.target.files[0]; if(!f) return; const r=new FileReader();
515:   r.onload=()=>{ try{ Object.assign(state, JSON.parse(r.result)); save(); renderAll(); }catch(err){ alert('Could not read that file.'); } };

517: });

## 👤 User Request (2026-06-12 22:17:54 UTC)

# Implementation Plan v2: Sensor Leak (done) + Full Fresh-Eyes Audit Findings

Updated by Claude (Fable 5) after independently repeating the same exercise: outline every module, trace each outline against the PRD (`docs/sophiaspace-prd.md`) + build dashboard intent, then walk backwards through the code. Verified against the codebase as of **v1.4.1 (build 9)**, 56/56 JUnit tests green.

## User Review Required

> [!NOTE]
> Item 1 (your original finding) is **already applied and verified** — it compiles and all tests pass, but it is **not yet released**: the APK on the phone/website (v1.4.1) predates the edit. Any further changes from this plan must ship through the release checklist at the bottom (this project versions every published build).

## Open Questions

None blocking. Items 5–6 are optional polish — Tom can veto.

## Status of the Original Plan

### ✅ [DONE — VERIFIED] [SensorSpikeScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/spike/SensorSpikeScreen.kt)
Your `DisposableEffect` → `LifecycleResumeEffect` migration is applied correctly (lines 55–58), matches the `SkyViewScreen` pattern, compiles, and the full suite passes. The finding was real: this was the one sensor consumer missed when the same bug was fixed in `SkyViewScreen` (v1.3.1). Good catch.

---

## New Findings (same audit method, deeper pass)

### 1. [MODIFY] [OrientationProvider.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/sky/OrientationProvider.kt) + [SkyViewScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt) — calibration prompt never fires (PRD-intent gap)
**Outline vs intent:** the PRD risk register specifies a *"kid-friendly figure-8 calibration ('draw a lazy 8 in the air!')"* in response to sensor noise. The script line exists (`SKY_SENSOR_ON` mentions 
<truncated 5405 bytes>
 pass; add a small JUnit for the nearest-planet selection if it's extracted into a pure helper (preferred).

### Manual (on the Pixel, wireless adb available at 192.168.50.67:38001 — port may rotate)
1. Item 1: in Sky View magic-window mode, wave the phone near a magnet/metal until accuracy drops — the lazy-8 line should play once, and not again within a minute.
2. Item 2: bump `SYNTH_VERSION`, reinstall, confirm sounds still play (new files render) and old `synth_*.wav` are gone from cache.
3. Item 3: tonight Venus and Jupiter are both up — point between them; the ring should claim the closer one.
4. Item 7: dashboard renders, Phase 0 shows the new ticks, no console errors.

## 🔒 Project guardrails (non-negotiable — from the PRD + research brief)
- **Pedagogy:** never an Earth-shadow/creeping-disc moon visual; the Sun never "moves/sets/sleeps" (we spin); too-hot/too-cold stays in habitable-zone "lived/always" language — never weather/season words; no fail-states (misses are gags).
- **Privacy:** CAMERA stays the only permission; preview-only, never stored/sent; no internet/location/accounts.
- **Release checklist for ANY shipped change:** bump `versionCode` + `versionName` in `app/build.gradle.kts` → `gradlew testDebugUnitTest assembleDebug` → copy APK to `Y:\ReynoldsFamily\builds\SophiasSpace-<version>.apk` (never overwrite a published version) → update the site card's version chip + download href in `Y:\ReynoldsFamily\builds\index.html` → append a dated entry to the dashboard `CHANGES` array → `adb install -r` to the Pixel. Next free slot: **v1.4.2 (build 10)** — note your already-applied SensorSpike fix rides in this release.
- **Site caveat:** `Y:\...\index.html` is sometimes regenerated by an external site build — re-read before editing, and know that the download-popup bypass fix (`programmaticDownload` flag) and the Sophia's Space card must survive any regeneration.

94: private fun planetFact(p: Ephemeris.Planet): String = when (p) {
95:     Ephemeris.Planet.MERCURY -> "That's Mercury! The smallest, speediest planet — it zips all the way around the Sun in just three months!"
96:     Ephemeris.Planet.VENUS -> "That's Venus — the brightest planet of all, wrapped in shiny clouds! People call it the evening star."
97:     Ephemeris.Planet.MARS -> "That's Mars, the rusty red planet! Remember catching it with your rocket?"
98:     Ephemeris.Planet.EARTH -> "That's home!" // never drawn — geocentric Earth is filtered out
99:     Ephemeris.Planet.JUPITER -> "That's Jupiter — the BIGGEST planet of all! A giant ball of swirling, stripey storms."
100:     Ephemeris.Planet.SATURN -> "That's Saturn, the one with the beautiful rings! They're made of a billion little pieces of ice."
101:     Ephemeris.Planet.URANUS -> "That's Uranus, hiding way out in the cold. It rolls around the Sun lying on its side!"
102:     Ephemeris.Planet.NEPTUNE -> "That's Neptune, the farthest one of all — the windiest, bluest planet!"
103: }
104: 
105: private const val SCREEN_FOV_DIVISOR = 1.2f // ~60° on the dark-sky view
106: /** Fallback tan(half-vertical-FOV) for AR if lens intrinsics are unreadable (~70°). */
107: private const val DEFAULT_AR_TAN_HALF_V = 0.70f
108: /** Planets are point targets — a tighter dwell radius than star figures.

116: @androidx.annotation.OptIn(ExperimentalCamera2Interop::class)
117: @Composable
118: fun SkyViewScreen(
119:     onBack: () -> Unit,
120:     narrator: NarrationManager,
121:     settings: AppSettings,
122:     modifier: Modifier = Modifier
123: ) {
124:     val context = LocalContext.current
125:     val catalog = remember { SkyCatalog.loadFromAssets(context) }
126:     val orientation = remember { OrientationProvider(context) }
127:     val lat by settings.homeLatitude.collectAsState(initial = AppSettings.DEFAULT_LAT)
128:     val lon by settings.homeLongitude.collectAsState(initial = AppSettings.DEFAULT_LON)
129:     val found by settings.foundConstellations.collectAsState(initial = emptySet())
130:     val allStickers by settings.stickers.collectAsState(initial = emptySet())
131: 
132:     var mode by remember { mutableStateOf(SkyMode.DRAG) }
133:     var dragAlt by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(35f) }
134:     var dragAz by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(180f) }
135:     val hasCamera = remember {
136:         context.packageManager.hasSystemFeature(PackageManager.FEATURE_CAMERA_ANY)
137:     }
138:     var cameraGranted by remember {
139:         mutableStateOf(
140:             ContextCompat.checkSelfPermission(context, Manifest.permission.CAMERA) ==
141:                 PackageManager.PERMISSION_GRANTED
142:         )
143:     }
144:     val pointing by orientation.pointing.collectAsState()
145:     var frameTick by remember { mutableLongStateOf(0L) }
146:     var confetti by remember { mutableIntStateOf(0) }
147:     var dwellTargetId by remember { mutableStateOf<String?>(null) }
148:     var dwellFrac by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(0f) }
149:     var revealId by remember { mutableStateOf<String?>(null) }

1: # Implementation Plan: Fix Background Sensor Resource Leak
2: 
3: Address background battery drain/sensor leak inside the Phase-0 diagnostic screen (`SensorSpikeScreen`).
4: 
5: ## User Review Required
6: 
7: > [!NOTE]
8: > This change is minor and has no user-visible UI impact, but it preserves battery life when the app goes to the background while viewing the developer tools/diagnostics screen.
9: 
10: ## Open Questions
11: 
12: None.
13: 
14: ## Proposed Changes
15: 
16: ### UI Components
17: 
18: #### [MODIFY] [SensorSpikeScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/spike/SensorSpikeScreen.kt)
19: Migrate the `OrientationProvider` registration from `DisposableEffect` to `LifecycleResumeEffect` so that the sensor is unregistered when the Activity is paused (backgrounded) and re-registered when resumed, matching the `SkyViewScreen` lifecycle behavior.
20: 
21: ---
22: 
23: ## Verification Plan
24: 
25: ### Automated Tests
26: Run unit tests to ensure no regressions are introduced:
27: - `.\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest`
28: 
29: ### Manual Verification
30: 1. Open the **Grown-up Corner** (2-second hold gate).
31: 2. Open **Open sensor spike 🧭**.
32: 3. Verify the sensor is actively reading and the needle rotates.
33: 4. Press home/minimize the app.
34: 5. Verify (via Logcat or CPU profiler) that no sensor events are processed in the background.
35: 6. Re-open the app and verify sensor tracking resumes immediately.
36:

13: 
14: /** Where the back of the phone is pointing, in sky coordinates. */
15: data class Pointing(
16:     /** Degrees clockwise from magnetic north, 0..360. */
17:     val azimuthDeg: Float,
18:     /** Degrees above the horizon, -90..90. */
19:     val altitudeDeg: Float
20: )
21: 
22: /**
23:  * Rotation-vector sensor → "magic window" look direction.
24:  *
25:  * The look vector is the device's -Z axis (out the back of the phone) expressed
26:  * in world ENU coordinates: hold the phone up like a window and the reported
27:  * azimuth/altitude is the patch of sky behind it. The raw vector is smoothed
28:  * with an exponential moving average and re-normalized — smoothing Euler angles
29:  * directly causes the classic 359°→0° snap.
30:  *
31:  * Register in onResume / unregister in onPause; the sensor must never run
32:  * while the screen is off (battery).
33:  */
34: class OrientationProvider(context: Context) : SensorEventListener {
35: 
36:     private val sensorManager = context.getSystemService(Context.SENSOR_SERVICE) as SensorManager
37:     private val rotationSensor
<truncated 1089 bytes>
5: 
66:     fun stop() {
67:         sensorManager.unregisterListener(this)
68:         primed = false
69:         _pointing.value = null
70:     }
71: 
72:     override fun onSensorChanged(event: SensorEvent) {
73:         if (event.sensor.type != Sensor.TYPE_ROTATION_VECTOR) return
74:         SensorManager.getRotationMatrixFromVector(rotationMatrix, event.values)
75: 
76:         // World-frame direction of the device -Z axis (out the back of the phone).
77:         // getRotationMatrixFromVector returns R with world = R * device, row-major.
78:         val lookX = -rotationMatrix[2]
79:         val lookY = -rotationMatrix[5]
80:         val lookZ = -rotationMatrix[8]
81: 
82:         if (!primed) {
83:             smoothX = lookX; smoothY = lookY; smoothZ = lookZ
84:             primed = true
85:         } else {
86:             val k = smoothing
87:             smoothX += k * (lookX - smoothX)
88:             smoothY += k * (lookY - smoothY)
89:             smoothZ += k * (lookZ - smoothZ)
90:         }
91:         val len = sqrt(smoothX * smoothX + smoothY * smoothY + smoothZ * smoothZ)
92:         if (len < 1e-6f) return
93:         val nx = smoothX / len
94:         val ny = smoothY / len
95:         val nz = smoothZ / len
96: 
97:         // World frame: X = east, Y = magnetic north, Z = up.
98:         val azimuth = Math.toDegrees(atan2(nx.toDouble(), ny.toDouble())).toFloat().let {
99:             if (it < 0f) it + 360f else it
100:         }
101:         val altitude = Math.toDegrees(asin(nz.toDouble().coerceIn(-1.0, 1.0))).toFloat()
102: 
103:         _pointing.value = Pointing(azimuth, altitude)
104:     }
105: 
106:     override fun onAccuracyChanged(sensor: Sensor?, accuracy: Int) = Unit
107: 
108:     companion object {
109:         const val DEFAULT_SMOOTHING = 0.18f
110:         /** Snappier tracking for camera AR — the overlay must keep up. */
111:         const val AR_SMOOTHING = 0.45f
112:     }
113: }

54:     SANDBOX_SUN_INTRO("Here's the Sun and its family! Everybody is circling the Sun. Try dragging a planet and see what happens!"),

14: /**
15:  * Tiny 8-bit-style synthesizer for interaction juice (SophiaQuest lineage).
16:  *
17:  * The six effects are rendered to small WAV files in the cache dir once, then
18:  * played through a single shared [SoundPool] — which owns the thread/voice
19:  * pool, enforces a concurrency cap, and applies volume live. (The previous
20:  * thread-per-sound AudioTrack scheme could exhaust the device's AudioTrack
21:  * limit under a five-year-old's tap rate, and leaked tracks on error paths.)
22:  *
23:  * The NarrationManager ducks this while speaking via [volumeScale].
24:  */
25: object SoundSynth {
26:     private const val SAMPLE_RATE = 22050
27: 
28:     @Volatile
29:     var volumeScale: Float = 1.0f
30: 
31:     private enum class Effect { POP, WHOOSH, SIZZLE, DRIFT, CHIME, FANFARE }
32: 
33:     @Volatile
34:     private var pool: SoundPool? = null
35:     private val soundIds = ConcurrentHashMap<Effect, Int>()
36:     private val readySamples = ConcurrentHashMap<Int, Boolean>()
37: 
38:     /** Call once from MainActivity.onCreate. Idempotent; renders off-thread. */
39:     fun init(context: Context) {
40:
<truncated 5644 bytes>
t) * 12000.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
176:         }
177:         return buffer
178:     }
179: 
180:     private fun renderFanfare(): ShortArray {
181:         val numSamples = samples(600)
182:         val buffer = ShortArray(numSamples)
183:         for (i in 0 until numSamples) {
184:             val t = i.toDouble() / SAMPLE_RATE
185:             val noteIdx = i / (numSamples / 4)
186:             val freq = when (noteIdx) {
187:                 0 -> 523.25
188:                 1 -> 783.99
189:                 2 -> 659.25
190:                 else -> 1046.50
191:             }
192:             val vibrato = if (noteIdx == 3) sin(2.0 * PI * 12.0 * t) * 8.0 else 0.0
193:             val envelope = 1.0 - (i.toDouble() / numSamples)
194:             buffer[i] = (sin(2.0 * PI * (freq + vibrato) * t) * 12000.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
195:         }
196:         return buffer
197:     }
198: 
199:     private fun samples(durationMs: Int) = (SAMPLE_RATE * (durationMs / 1000.0)).toInt()
200: 
201:     /** Minimal 16-bit mono PCM WAV writer. */
202:     private fun writeWav(file: File, pcm: ShortArray) {
203:         DataOutputStream(BufferedOutputStream(FileOutputStream(file))).use { o ->
204:             fun le32(v: Int) {
205:                 o.write(v and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 8) and 0xFF)
206:                 o.write((v shr 16) and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 24) and 0xFF)
207:             }
208:             fun le16(v: Int) {
209:                 o.write(v and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 8) and 0xFF)
210:             }
211:             val dataSize = pcm.size * 2
212:             o.writeBytes("RIFF"); le32(36 + dataSize); o.writeBytes("WAVE")
213:             o.writeBytes("fmt "); le32(16); le16(1); le16(1)
214:             le32(SAMPLE_RATE); le32(SAMPLE_RATE * 2); le16(2); le16(16)
215:             o.writeBytes("data"); le32(dataSize)
216:             for (s in pcm) le16(s.toInt() and 0xFFFF)
217:         }
218:     }
219: }

16: private class TwinkleStar(val fx: Float, val fy: Float, val size: Float, val phase: Float, val speed: Float)
17: 
18: /**
19:  * Gently twinkling star backdrop drawn behind every screen.
20:  * Stars are generated once per composition; only their alpha animates.
21:  */
22: @Composable
23: fun SpaceBackground(modifier: Modifier = Modifier, starCount: Int = 90) {
24:     val stars = remember {
25:         val rng = Random(42)
26:         List(starCount) {
27:             TwinkleStar(
28:                 fx = rng.nextFloat(),
29:                 fy = rng.nextFloat(),
30:                 size = 1.2f + rng.nextFloat() * 2.2f,
31:                 phase = rng.nextFloat() * 6.2832f,
32:                 speed = 0.4f + rng.nextFloat() * 0.9f
33:             )
34:         }
35:     }
36: 
37:     var time by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(0f) }
38:     LaunchedEffect(Unit) {
39:         val start = System.nanoTime()
40:         while (true) {
41:             androidx.compose.runtime.withFrameNanos { now ->
42:                 time = (now - start) / 1_000_000_000f
43:             }
44:         }
45:     }
46: 
47:     Canvas(modifier = modifier) {
48:         for (s in stars) {
49:             val alpha = 0.25f + 0.55f * (0.5f + 0.5f * sin(time * s.speed + s.phase))
50:             drawCircle(
51:                 color = Color.White.copy(alpha = alpha),
52:                 radius = s.size,
53:                 center = Offset(s.fx * size.width, s.fy * size.height)
54:             )
55:         }
56:     }
57: }

  "CodeContent": "\"# Implementation Plan: Audit Findings and release v1.4.2 (build 10)\\n\\nAddress seven audit findings and enhancements verified against the codebase as of v1.4.1 (build 9), then execute the release checklist for v1.4.2 (build 10).\\n\\n## User Review Required\\n\\n> [!NOTE]\\n> All changes follow strict pedagogical and privacy constraints: no Earth-shadow graphics, fixed-Sun Day/Night, habitable-zone wording, local-only Camera preview.\\n> The next release is versioned as **v1.4.2 (build 10)**.\\n\\n## Open Questions\\n\\nNone.\\n\\n## Proposed Changes\\n\\n### Core Sensor and Calibration\\n\\n#### [MODIFY] [OrientationProvider.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/sky/OrientationProvider.kt)\\n- Add private `_accuracy` flow initialized with `SensorManager.SENSOR_STATUS_ACCURACY_HIGH`.\\n- Expose `accuracy: StateFlow<Int> = _accuracy`.\\n- Capture accuracy update in `onAccuracyChanged` for `Sensor.TYPE_ROTATION_VECTOR`.\\n\\n#### [MODIFY] [Scripts.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/Scripts.kt)\\n- Add `SKY_CALIBRATE` spoken line: *\\\"Hmm, the compass feels dizzy! Draw a big lazy eight in the air with the phone!\\\"*\\n\\n#### [MODIFY] [SkyViewScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt)\\n- Collect `accuracy` state from `OrientationProvider`.\\n- Track `lastCalibratePromptTime` state.\\n- In `LaunchedEffect(accuracy, mode)`, if in SENSOR/AR mode and accuracy drops to low/unreliable, speak `SKY_CALIBRATE` debounced to 60s.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### WAV Cache Versioning\\n\\n#### [MODIFY] [SoundSynth.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/SoundSynth.kt)\\n- Add `private const val SYNTH_VERSION = 2`.\\n- Change cached filename pattern to `synth_v${SYNTH_VERSION}_<name>.wav`.\\n- On initialization, scan \n<truncated 4372 bytes>",

145:     val pointing by orientation.pointing.collectAsState()
146:     var frameTick by remember { mutableLongStateOf(0L) }
147:     var confetti by remember { mutableIntStateOf(0) }
148:     var dwellTargetId by remember { mutableStateOf<String?>(null) }
149:     var dwellFrac by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(0f) }
150:     var revealId by remember { mutableStateOf<String?>(null) }
151:     var revealProgress by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(0f) }
152:     // After a find, stay quiet until the reticle leaves that figure.
153:     var cooldownId by remember { mutableStateOf<String?>(null) }
154:     val scope = rememberCoroutineScope()
155: 
156:     // The rotation sensor reports azimuth from MAGNETIC north; the star math
157:     // uses true north. Correct with the home city's declination — free, no
158:     // location permission (in NJ this is ~12.5°, bigger than the dwell radius).
159:     val declination = remember(lat, lon) {
160:         GeomagneticField(lat, lon, 100f, System.currentTimeMillis()).declination
161:     }
162:     val declinationState = rememberUpdatedState(declination)
163:     val textMeasurer = rememberTextMeasurer()
164: 
165:     // The camera permission ask — fired the first time she enters Real-sky.
166:     // Denied: quietly stay in the magic window; the chip stays retryable.
167:     val cameraPermissionLauncher = rememberLauncherForActivityResult(
168:         ActivityResultContracts.RequestPermission()
169:     ) { granted ->
170:         cameraGranted = granted
171:         if (granted) {
172:             mode = SkyMode.AR
173:             narrator.speak(ScriptKey.SKY_AR_ON)
174:         }
175:     }
176: 
177:     val usingSensor = mode != SkyMode.DRAG
178:     // Sensor runs only while this screen is RESUMED with a sensor mode on —
179:     // never while the app is backgrounded (battery; PRD "unregister on pause").

  "ReplacementContent": "\"    private const val SAMPLE_RATE = 22050\\n    private const val SYNTH_VERSION = 2\\n\\n    @Volatile\\n    var volumeScale: Float = 1.0f\\n\\n    private enum class Effect { POP, WHOOSH, SIZZLE, DRIFT, CHIME, FANFARE }\\n\\n    @Volatile\\n    private var pool: SoundPool? = null\\n    private val soundIds = ConcurrentHashMap<Effect, Int>()\\n    private val readySamples = ConcurrentHashMap<Int, Boolean>()\\n\\n    /** Call once from MainActivity.onCreate. Idempotent; renders off-thread. */\\n    fun init(context: Context) {\\n        if (pool != null) return\\n        val p = SoundPool.Builder()\\n            .setMaxStreams(4)\\n            .setAudioAttributes(\\n                AudioAttributes.Builder()\\n                    .setUsage(AudioAttributes.USAGE_GAME)\\n                    .setContentType(AudioAttributes.CONTENT_TYPE_SONIFICATION)\\n                    .build()\\n            )\\n            .build()\\n        p.setOnLoadCompleteListener { _, sampleId, status ->\\n            if (status == 0) readySamples[sampleId] = true\\n        }\\n        pool = p\\n        val cacheDir = context.applicationContext.cacheDir\\n        // One short-lived render thread at startup \u2014 not one per tap.\\n        Thread {\\n            try {\\n                val prefix = \\\"synth_v${SYNTH_VERSION}_\\\"\\n                cacheDir.listFiles()?.forEach { file ->\\n                    if (file.name.startsWith(\\\"synth_\\\") && !file.name.startsWith(prefix)) {\\n                        file.delete()\\n                    }\\n                }\\n                for (effect in Effect.entries) {\\n                    val file = File(cacheDir, \\\"synth_v${SYNTH_VERSION}_${effect.name.lowercase()}.wav\\\")\\n                    if (!file.exists() || file.length() == 0L) writeWav(file, render(effect))\\n                    soundIds[effect] = p.load(file.absolutePath, 1)\\n                }\\n            } catch (e: Exception) {\\n                e.printStackTrace()\\n            }\\n        }.start()\\n    }\"",

 /** Planets are point targets — a tighter dwell radius than star figures. */
 private const val PLANET_FIND_RADIUS_DEG = 6.0
 
+/** Finds the nearest planet within the threshold. */
+fun findNearestPlanet(
+    planets: List<Triple<Ephemeris.Planet, Double, Double>>,
+    centerAlt: Double,
+    centerAz: Double,
+    maxRadiusDeg: Double = PLANET_FIND_RADIUS_DEG,
+    minAltitudeDeg: Double = MIN_FIND_ALTITUDE_DEG
+): Ephemeris.Planet? {
+    var minSep = Double.MAX_VALUE
+    var result: Ephemeris.Planet? = null
+    for ((pl, alt, az) in planets) {
+        if (alt <= minAltitudeDeg) continue
+        val sep = AstroMath.angularSeparationDeg(alt, az, centerAlt, centerAz)
+        if (sep < maxRadiusDeg && sep < minSep) {
+            minSep = sep
+            result = pl
+        }
+    }
+    return result
+}
+

1: package com.example.sophiaspace.sky
2: 
3: import org.junit.Assert.assertEquals
4: import org.junit.Assert.assertNull
5: import org.junit.Assert.assertTrue
6: import org.junit.Test
7: 
8: class AstroMathTest {
9: 
10:     // 2026-06-10 00:00:00 UTC in epoch millis.
11:     private val nowMillis = 1781049600000L
12: 
13:     @Test
14:     fun `julian day of the unix epoch is 2440587_5`() {
15:         assertEquals(2440587.5, AstroMath.julianDay(0L), 1e-9)
16:     }
17: 
18:     @Test
19:     fun `gmst is in 0-360 and gains a sidereal degree per day`() {
20:         val jd = AstroMath.julianDay(nowMillis)
21:         val g0 = AstroMath.gmstDeg(jd)
22:         val g1 = AstroMath.gmstDeg(jd + 1.0)
23:         assertTrue(g0 in 0.0..360.0)
24:         val advance = ((g1 - g0) % 360.0 + 360.0) % 360.0
25:         assertEquals(0.9856, advance, 0.01) // sidereal gain ≈ 0.9856°/day
26:     }
27: 
28:     @Test
29:     fun `polaris altitude is about the observer latitude, azimuth about north`() {
30:         // Polaris: RA 37.95°, Dec +89.26°. Observer at Denver-ish (39.7N, -105.0E).
31:         val jd = AstroMath.julianDay(nowMillis)
32:         val (alt, az) = AstroMath.altAz(37.95, 89.26, 39.7, -105.0, jd)
33:         assertEquals(39.7, alt, 1.5)
34:         assertTrue("azimuth should be near north: $az", az < 3.0 || az > 357.0)
35:     }
36: 
37:     @Test
38:     fun `a star on the celestial equator culminates at 90 minus latitude`() {
39:         var maxAlt = -90.0
40:         val jd0 = AstroMath.julianDay(nowMillis)
41:         for (m in 0 until 1440) {
42:             val (alt, _) = AstroMath.altAz(180.0, 0.0, 40.0, 0.0, jd0 + m / 1440.0)
43:             if (alt > maxAlt) maxAlt = alt
44:         }
45:         assertEquals(50.0, maxAlt, 0.5)
46:     }
47: 
48:     @Test
49:     fun `projection - the look-center lands at the origin and behind-camera culls`() {
50:         val center = AstroMath.project(45.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)
51:         assertEquals(0.0, center!!.first, 1e-9)
52:         assertEquals(0.0, center.second, 1e-9)
53:         assertNull(AstroMath.project(-45.0, 0.0, 45.0, 180.0))
54:     }
55: 
56:     @Test
57:     fun `projection - a star above the look-center projects upward`() {
58:         val p = AstroMath.project(55.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)!!
59:         assertTrue("higher star should have +y: ${p.second}", p.second > 0.0)
60:         assertEquals(0.0, p.first, 1e-6)
61:     }
62: 
63:     @Test
64:     fun `camera projection - center at origin, offsets are exact tangents, behind culls`() {
65:         val center = AstroMath.projectCamera(45.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)!!
66:         assertEquals(0.0, center.first, 1e-9)
67:         assertEquals(0.0, center.second, 1e-9)
68:         // 10° above the look-center must land at exactly tan(10°) —
69:         // that's what makes the overlay pan at the camera image's rate.
70:         val above = AstroMath.projectCamera(55.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)!!
71:         assertEquals(Math.tan(Math.toRadians(10.0)), above.second, 1e-6)
72:         assertEquals(0.0, above.first, 1e-6)
73:         assertNull(AstroMath.projectCamera(-45.0, 0.0, 45.0, 180.0))
74:     }
75: }

  "CodeContent": "\"package com.example.sophiaspace.ui.sky\\n\\nimport com.example.sophiaspace.sky.Ephemeris\\nimport org.junit.Assert.assertEquals\\nimport org.junit.Assert.assertNull\\nimport org.junit.Test\\n\\nclass SkyViewTest {\\n\\n    @Test\\n    fun `findNearestPlanet returns nearest planet within radius`() {\\n        val planets = listOf(\\n            Triple(Ephemeris.Planet.JUPITER, 12.0, 180.0),\\n            Triple(Ephemeris.Planet.VENUS, 14.0, 180.0)\\n        )\\n        val nearest = findNearestPlanet(planets, 10.0, 180.0)\\n        assertEquals(Ephemeris.Planet.JUPITER, nearest)\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    fun `findNearestPlanet returns nearest planet even if declaration order is reversed`() {\\n        val planets = listOf(\\n            Triple(Ephemeris.Planet.VENUS, 14.0, 180.0),\\n            Triple(Ephemeris.Planet.JUPITER, 12.0, 180.0)\\n        )\\n        val nearest = findNearestPlanet(planets, 10.0, 180.0)\\n        assertEquals(Ephemeris.Planet.JUPITER, nearest)\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    fun `findNearestPlanet returns null if all planets exceed max radius`() {\\n        val planets = listOf(\\n            Triple(Ephemeris.Planet.JUPITER, 20.0, 180.0)\\n        )\\n        val nearest = findNearestPlanet(planets, 10.0, 180.0, maxRadiusDeg = 6.0)\\n        assertNull(nearest)\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    fun `findNearestPlanet ignores planets below minimum altitude`() {\\n        val planets = listOf(\\n            Triple(Ephemeris.Planet.JUPITER, 1.0, 180.0)\\n        )\\n        val nearest = findNearestPlanet(planets, 2.0, 180.0, minAltitudeDeg = 5.0)\\n        assertNull(nearest)\\n    }\\n}\\n\"",
  "Description": "\"Create SkyViewTest.kt unit tests\"",
  "Overwrite": "true",
  "TargetFile": "\"c:\\\\Users\\\\thoma\\\\Dropbox\\\\My Documents\\\\Programs\\\\Solar System\\\\app\\\\app\\\\src\\\\test\\\\java\\\\com\\\\example\\\\sophiaspace\\\\ui\\\\sky\\\\SkyViewTest.kt\"",
  "toolAction": "\"Writing SkyViewTest.kt\"",
  "toolSummary": "\"Write SkyViewTest.kt\""
}
