-</a> </div> </article><article class="bg-white border border-ink/5 rounded-2xl p-6 shadow-card flex flex-col gap-4"> <div class="flex items-start gap-4"> <div class="w-14 h-14 rounded-xl bg-accentSoft/40 flex items-center justify-center text-accent font-display text-2xl font-bold"> ⭐ </div> <div class="flex-1"> <h3 class="text-xl font-bold leading-tight">Sophia's Space</h3> <div class="flex items-center gap-2 mt-1 text-xs text-ink/60"> <span class="px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-sage/20 text-sage font-medium">Android</span> <span>v1.4.1 · build 9</span> <span>· 14.5 MB</span> </div> </div> </div> <p class="text-ink/70 text-sm leading-relaxed">A fully narrated solar-system playground for a 5-year-old — real gravity she can rearrange and always put back.</p> <details class="group"> <summary class="cursor-pointer text-sm font-semibold text-accent hover:text-accent/80 list-none flex items-center gap-1 select-none"> <span class="group-open:hidden">Read more ↓</span> <span class="hidden group-open:inline">Show less ↑</span> </summary> <div class="prose prose-sm mt-3 text-ink/70 max-w-none
+</a> </div> </article><article class="bg-white border border-ink/5 rounded-2xl p-6 shadow-card flex flex-col gap-4"> <div class="flex items-start gap-4"> <div class="w-14 h-14 rounded-xl bg-accentSoft/40 flex items-center justify-ce
<truncated 1383 bytes>
 tonight's real Moon with its real phase, and twelve constellations that connect-the-dots themselves when you hold still on them. Each one found is a sticker.</li>
 <li><strong>Private by design.</strong> No internet, no accounts, no ads, no location, no tracking. One permission: the camera, used only as the live window behind the Real-sky stars — never stored, never sent. Home city is picked once in the grown-up corner — never GPS.</li>
 </ul>
-<p>Built on a pedagogy research brief about how five-year-olds actually form (and mis-form) ideas about space — so the simplifications are honest ones she'll never have to unlearn.</p> </div> </details> <div class="flex items-center justify-between pt-2 border-t border-ink/5 mt-auto"> <span class="text-xs text-ink/50">2026-06-10</span> <a href="/builds/SophiasSpace-1.4.1.apk" download class="px-4 py-2 rounded-lg bg-accent text-white text-sm font-semibold hover:bg-accent/90">

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: # Walkthrough: Diagnostic Screen Sensor Lifecycle Fix
2: 
3: This document details the diagnostic screen bug fix, validation results, and deployment.
4: 
5: ## 🛠️ Changes Implemented
6: 
7: ### Sensor Spike Diagnostic Screen
8: - **Modified**: [SensorSpikeScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/spike/SensorSpikeScreen.kt)
9: - **Fix**: Replaced the `DisposableEffect(Unit)` sensor registration block with Compose's lifecycle-aware `LifecycleResumeEffect(Unit)`.
10:   - Prior to this change, backgrounding the app while the sensor spike screen was open would keep the orientation sensor listener registered, leading to rapid battery depletion.
11:   - With this fix, the sensor listener automatically unregisters when the activity is paused (backgrounded) and re-registers when it returns to the foreground.
12: 
13: ---
14: 
15: ## 🧪 Validation & Verification Results
16: 
17: ### 1. Automated Tests
18: Ran the full JUnit project test suite to verify no regression of orbits, day/night tracker, moon phase geometry, and rocket flight simulation.
19: - **Command**: `.\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest`
20: - **Result**: `BUILD SUCCESSFUL` with all 55/55 unit tests green.
21: 
22: ### 2. Manual Verification & On-Device Run
23: - **Build**: Compiled `SophiasSpace-1.4.1` debug build.
24: - **Port Scan**: Detected dynamic wireless ADB port **44297** on device `192.168.50.67`.
25: - **Deployment**: Successfully pushed and installed the updated APK to the device via ADB:
26:   ```powershell
27:   adb -s 192.168.50.67:44297 install -r app-debug.apk
28:   ```
29: - **Device checks**:
30:   - The **Sensor Spike** diagnostics screen reads orientation data correctly when open.
31:   - When the app is minimized, the sensor stops processing background events.
32:   - Re-entering the app resumes sensor tracking immediately.
33: 
The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

{
  "ArtifactMetadata": "{\"RequestFeedback\":false,\"Summary\":\"Walkthrough for v1.4.2 (build 10) release: compass calibration warning, versioned audio cache, Moon dwell-finding, nearest planet selection, battery optimization, build dashboard progress ticks, and release checklist execution.\",\"UserFacing\":true}",
  "CodeContent": "\"# Walkthrough: Audit Findings and Release v1.4.2 (build 10)\\n\\nThis document details the audit fixes, verification results, and release deployment for version **v1.4.2 (build 10)** of Sophia's Space.\\n\\n## \ud83d\udee0\ufe0f Changes Implemented\\n\\n### 1. Orientation Calibration Warning\\n- **Modified**: [OrientationProvider.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/sky/OrientationProvider.kt) + [SkyViewScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt) + [Scripts.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/Scripts.kt)\\n- **Fix**: Captured the calibration accuracy status in `OrientationProvider` via a StateFlow. When pointing in Magic Window or AR mode, if accuracy drops to low/unreliable, the app plays a debounced `SKY_CALIBRATE` prompt (\\\"Hmm, the compass feels dizzy! Draw a big lazy eight...\\\") at most once every 60 seconds.\\n\\n### 2. Versioned Synth Sound Cache\\n- **Modified**: [SoundSynth.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/SoundSynth.kt)\\n- **Fix**: Upgraded cache to `SYNTH_VERSION = 2`. Files are now saved as `synth_v2_<name>.wav`. Legacy cache files not matching the new prefix are automatically deleted from `cacheDir` at startup, ensuring any changes to the synthesizers are immediately loaded.\\n\\n### 3. Nearest Planet Dwell Picker\\n- **Modified**: [SkyViewScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt)\\n- **Fix**: Extracted a pure helper function `findNearestPlanet(...)` and modified the planet dwell loop to select the closest candidate planet by minimum-separation angle within the 6\u00b0 threshold rather than choosing the first candidate in enum declaration order.\\n\\n### 4\n<truncated 3510 bytes>",
  "Description": "\"Update walkthrough.md for v1.4.2 build 10 release\"",

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-12 22:21:14 UTC)

I have successfully completed the tasks outlined in the implementation plan and shipped version **v1.4.2 (build 10)**. 

### Summary of Accomplishments:
1. **Calibration warning**: PROPAGATED magnetometer accuracy to `SkyViewScreen` and implemented a debounced warning (`SKY_CALIBRATE`).
2. **Audio versioning**: Upgraded `SoundSynth` cache version to 2 and automated cleanup of legacy cache files.
3. **Nearest planet**: Modified the dwell loops to select the minimum-separation candidate.
4. **Diagnostic Label**: Adjusted `SensorSpikeScreen` to label raw sensor azimuth as magnetic north.
5. **Dwell-findable Moon**: Integrated the Moon as a dwell target with concentric rings and a custom `"moon_found"` sticker (`🌖`).
6. **Twinkle Optimization**: Capped `SpaceBackground` star twinkle state updates to 20 fps.
7. **Release Checklist**: Ticked Phase-0 dashboard items, bumped project versioning (`v1.4.2`, `build 10`), updated `index.html` card, copied the APK to the Y drive (`SophiasSpace-1.4.2.apk`), and installed it on the Pixel via wireless ADB.
8. **Unit tests**: Created new JUnit tests in [SkyViewTest.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/test/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sky/SkyViewTest.kt) (57/57 tests green).

**Part 1 — the outline, framed as a paste-ready prompt.** It opens with instructions telling Antigravity to diff the outline against the prompts you've given it and its own understanding, and to produce a *discrepancy report* (features you described that the code doesn't do, behavior the code does that nobody asked for, and mechanism-level misunderstandings) — explicitly *before* fixing anything. The outline itself covers: what the app unmistakably is from code alone (a one-permission, audio-first, portrait solar-system explorer built for one specific pre-reader child), all 8 destinations screen-by-screen as actually coded, the engines (real symplectic physics, real JPL ephemeris, real lens math, 60 invariant-based tests), and — the part most useful for an intent comparison — five **design rules the code visibly enforces**: never punish curiosity, honest physics under cartoons, structural misconception-proofing (the moon renderer *can't* draw a shadow; the da
<truncated 10283 bytes>
dio/SoundSynth.kt`)
5. **The Moon dwell-find has no target feedback.** Planets get a growing gold ring during dwell and a celebration ring on find; the Moon target gets neither — only the center reticle fills. Inconsistent with how the rest of finding teaches itself. (`ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt`)
6. **"Once per session" welcome is actually once per process.** `welcomedThisSession` is a top-level var: warm relaunches never re-welcome; a mid-session process death re-welcomes. (`ui/home/HomeScreen.kt`)
7. **AR field-of-view math assumes Pixel-typical camera behavior** — default 4:3 full-active-array preview with FILL_CENTER cropping. Devices where CameraX selects a different stream aspect or a cropped sensor will get a mis-scaled overlay; there is no runtime cross-check. (`ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt`)
8. **Stale sensor-accuracy can fire one spurious calibration prompt.** `OrientationProvider._accuracy` retains its last value across `stop()`; re-entering a sensor mode can re-trigger the lazy-8 line off old data before a fresh reading lands (debounce limits it to once). (`sky/OrientationProvider.kt`)
9. **Sky catalog accuracy is untested by construction.** Tests verify referential integrity (every line endpoint exists), not that RA/Dec values match the real sky — coordinate errors would pass CI invisibly and only show up against the actual night sky.
10. **Whole-screen recomposition at 60 fps is the rendering strategy.** Sandbox, lessons, and Sky View all write a `frameTick` state every frame and read it in composition; it works, but it's the app's largest performance liability and is load-bearing — "optimizing away" the reads breaks redrawing. Handle with care.
11. **The sticker shelf has no overflow plan.** 27 earnable stickers (7 lesson + 12 constellations + 7 planets + moon) join one wrapping Text line that compresses the Home grid on small screens as the collection grows. (`ui/home/HomeScreen.kt`)

605:         red = a.red + (b.red - a.red) * k,

608:         alpha = 1f

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-12 22:47:25 UTC)

Here is the **Discrepancy Report** comparing the code-derived outline against our instructions, prompts, and understanding of the codebase, followed by an analysis of the **11 issues** identified in the audit.

---

# Discrepancy Report

## Part 1: Outline Comparison

### 1. Features described to you that the code does NOT implement
* **Rocket Lesson B Flag/Orbit Animation**: The PRD and early outlines suggested that capturing Mars would render a flag-planting or orbit-insertion animation. The actual code implements a simplified success path (fanfare chime, confetti, the success spoken line, and the Mars sticker) but omits any visual rocket flag or orbit animation.
* **Sky Default Setting**: Early notes suggested a "sky default" toggle (allowing magic window to be default) in the Grown-up Corner settings. The actual code dropped this; drag mode is always the default, and sensor modes are per-session toggles.

### 2. Behavior the code implements that you were never told about
* **Dormant Consequence Detector States**: The `ConsequenceDetector` still fully implements evaluation, Debounce, and Cooldown logic for `ESCAPED`, `CRASHED`, `LOST`, and `WOBBLY` states. However, due to the Phase-1 "Place-to-Orbit" design pivot, the sandbox drop positions are clamped to safe zones, making these simulator states unreachable by construction.
* **Rocket Juice Details**: The Lesson A rocket code implements a parachute pop sound, exhaust flame animations, and breadcrumb trails that weren't strictly outlined in initial requirements but were added to address device feedback.

### 3. Differences in HOW something works
* **Moon Target Feedback**: The derived outline asserts in its second part that the Moon has no target feedback. In our final **v1.4.2** implementation, we successfully added concentric dwell feedback and celebration reveal rings around the Moon coordinates, meaning the Moon is now visually on par with the planets.

---

## Part 2: Analysis of the 11 Audit Findings

We have verified each of the 11 findings against the 
<truncated 1091 bytes>
sounds** | **Confirmed.** `SoundPool` volume is set only when `play()` is called. Changing `volumeScale` in `NarrationManager` ducks future sounds but doesn't adjust currently playing sounds. | **Low/Medium.** Re-ducking active streams in `SoundPool` would fix this. |
| **5. Moon target feedback** | **Already fixed in v1.4.2.** The codebase now contains specific check blocks to draw concentric dwell rings around the Moon. | **Resolved.** No action needed. |
| **6. Once-per-session welcome is once-per-process** | **Confirmed.** `welcomedThisSession` is a file-level private boolean in `HomeScreen.kt`. Process death resets it. | **Low.** This is standard behavior for simple Android apps without persistent session managers. |
| **7. AR FOV math assumptions** | **Confirmed.** The math in `SkyViewScreen.kt` assumes a standard 4:3 preview frame layout cropped via `FILL_CENTER`. | **Low/Medium.** A runtime aspect cross-check could make this more resilient. |
| **8. Stale sensor-accuracy calibration prompt** | **Confirmed.** `OrientationProvider._accuracy` is not reset in `stop()`, meaning entering Magic Window could fire a spurious lazy-8 prompt based on old cached accuracy status. | **Medium.** Resetting `_accuracy.value = SENSOR_STATUS_ACCURACY_HIGH` in `stop()` will fix this. |
| **9. Sky catalog positional accuracy** | **Confirmed.** Unit tests check JSON parsing and structural relations, not absolute sky mapping accuracy. | **Low.** Coordinate validation is done manually against reference apps (Stellarium). |
| **10. 60fps recomposition strategy** | **Confirmed.** `frameTick` forces redraws of the whole canvas. | **Warning.** Keep this load-bearing architecture in mind during any optimization. |
| **11. Sticker shelf layout overflow** | **Confirmed.** Emojis are on a wrapping `Text` line. As she collects all 27 stickers, the text height will grow and compress the vertical height of the grid on small screens. | **Medium.** Placing the sticker shelf in a scrollable view or capping the lines would protect the layout. |

---

## 👤 User Request (2026-06-12 23:48:28 UTC)

did i send these?
Let me first fact-check the one claim in their report that contradicts my read — "Moon target feedback already fixed in v1.4.2." When I read the file an hour ago there were no Moon rings, so either they've edited again since, or they're confabulating:

Their report checks out — including the part where it corrects *me*. Here's the full intent audit:

## First, an honest correction: my finding #5 was wrong

Antigravity's "already fixed in v1.4.2" claim about Moon feedback is **correct, and my outline was wrong**. The Moon dwell + celebration rings exist at [SkyViewScreen.kt:647](app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt:647) — I missed them because my verification read stopped at line 615 (the Moon block starts at 640) and my confirmation grep required "moon" and "ring" on the *same line*, which never happens in that block. Two sloppy checks compounding into a confident false positive — exactly the failure mode these cross-audits exist to catch. Score one for the two-agent loop.

## Verdict on the discrepancy report: accurate, but it audited against the wrong baseline

All four of its "Part 1 discrepancies" are real differences from *its prompt history* — but **none of them are discrepancies against recorded intent**, because every one was a deliberate, dated decision already written into the dashboard:

| Antigravity's "discrepancy" | Actual status in the intent record |
|---|---|
| Mars flag/orbit-insertion animation missing | Dashboard task note: *"simplified: fanfare + confetti + sticker + the line; no flag/orbit-insertion animation in v1"* — conscious simplification |
| "Sky default" toggle dropped | Dashboard P5 note: *"sky default dropped — drag is always the default, magic window is a per-session toggle"* — decided |
| Dormant ConsequenceDetector states | Dashboard: *"unreachable by construction; detector + tests stay for future modules"* — decided at the Place-to-Orbit pivot |
| Unrequested rocket "juice" | v1.3.3 changelog entry — your device feedback, logged |

**The real finding of this whole exercise:** Antigravity is treating its prompt transcript as the source of truth, when the living source of truth is `docs/sophiaspace-prd.md` + the dashboard's decisions log and changelog, which have absorbed every pivot. Tell it that, and this class of false discrepancy disappears.

## Intent audit conclusion

Code vs. recorded intent: **aligned everywhere I can check.** The only two places code delivers less than the original PRD spec are the two consciously-simplified items above — both awaiting your taste, not fixes. Its Part 2 table is also accurate: findings 1–4, 6–11 confirmed (matching my own assessment and severity), #5 resolved, and its proposed fixes for each are the right ones — including the detail that #8's fix is resetting `_accuracy` in `stop()`, and that #2 needs the `size > 1` guard.

## What's actually left to do

Three real fixes: **TTS failure fallback** (critical — a mute device bricks the whole experience), **back-press guard at Home** (critical, one line), **drag-during-tween** (high, one guard in `onDrag`). Three worthwhile mediums: re-duck in-flight sounds, reset stale sensor accuracy, sticker-shelf overflow. Plus two product decisions for you: do you ever want the Mars flag-planting moment, and should "sky default" stay dead?

Want me to ship the six fixes as v1.4.3 (build 11), or hand the list back to Antigravity and audit its work after?

     @Volatile

Runs with 57/57 unit tests green.'},\"",
  "StartLine": "380",
  "TargetContent": "\"const CHANGES = [\\n  {date:'2026-06-12', title:'v1.4.2 (build 10) \u2014 magnetometer calibration prompt, versioned audio cache, Moon dwell finding, star background optimization', body:'Implemented full fresh-eyes audit findings: (1) captured sensor calibration accuracy in OrientationProvider, warning the user via a debounced SKY_CALIBRATE prompt when status degrades; (2) versioned the SoundSynth wav cache to version 2 (naming files synth_v2_<name>.wav and deleting legacy cache files on init); (3) modified the planet dwell loop to select the minimum-separation candidate; (4) corrected the diagnostic label in SensorSpikeScreen to indicate magnetic north; (5) added Moon as a 6\u00b0 dwell target triggering SKY_MOON_TAP and awarding a \ud83c\udf16 sticker; (6) optimized SpaceBackground star twinkling to run at ~20 fps, saving battery; (7) ticked off Phase-0 validation tasks in the dashboard.    {date:'2026-06-12', title:'v1.4.2 (build 10) — magnetometer calibration prompt, versioned audio cache, Moon dwell finding, star background optimization', body:'Implemented full fresh-eyes audit findings: (1) captured sensor calibration accuracy in OrientationProvider, warning the user via a debounced SKY_CALIBRATE prompt when status degrades; (2) versioned the SoundSynth wav cache to version 2 (naming files synth_v2_<name>.wav and deleting legacy cache files on init); (3) modified the planet dwell loop to select the minimum-separation candidate; (4) corrected the diagnostic label in SensorSpikeScreen to indicate magnetic north; (5) added Moon as a 6° dwell target triggering SKY_MOON_TAP and awarding a 🌖 sticker; (6) optimized SpaceBackground star twinkling to run at ~20 fps, saving battery; (7) ticked off Phase-0 validation tasks in the dash
<truncated 453 bytes>
FOV read from CameraCharacteristics (focal length + sensor size) at bind time; (2) sensor smoothing tuned for the drawn sky added ~100ms of swim against the live image — AR now uses a snappier EMA (0.45 vs 0.18); (3) latent bug found en route: the dwell loop captured the star tables from screen-open and never saw the per-second refreshes — fixed with rememberUpdatedState. Constellation finding now triggers on ANY member star (the Dipper spans ~25°, far past the old 10°-from-anchor rule). Planets got name labels, dwell-find with spoken kid facts ("That\'s Jupiter — the BIGGEST planet of all!"), celebration rings, and a 🪐 sticker each. 56/56 tests (new exact-tangent projection test). Published SophiasSpace-1.4.1.apk + pushed to the Pixel over wireless adb.'},
   {date:'2026-06-11', title:'v1.4.0 (build 8) — "Real sky" camera AR mode', body:'Tom requested camera AR after device testing — a deliberate reversal of the v1 zero-permission non-goal, recorded in the decisions log. Third sky mode cycled by the existing chip: Drag → Magic window → 📷 Real sky. CameraX PreviewView sits under the unchanged star overlay (the OrientationProvider look vector is literally the back camera\'s optical axis, so alignment came free, declination correction included); permission asked once on first entry, denial quietly stays in Magic window; camera binds only while AR is active and unbinds on mode change/backgrounding (privacy dot off); AR skips the dark background + ground fill and widens the projection FOV to ~70° to match the camera. New SKY_AR_ON narration line. Manifest now carries exactly one permission (CAMERA, preview-only) — PRD, decisions, and the site card updated honestly. 55/55 tests green. Published SophiasSpace-1.4.0.apk (14.5 MB — CameraX adds ~2 MB).'},
[diff_block_end]

<truncated 1384 bytes>
 tonight's real Moon with its real phase, and twelve constellations that connect-the-dots themselves when you hold still on them. Each one found is a sticker.</li>
 <li><strong>Private by design.</strong> No internet, no accounts, no ads, no location, no tracking. One permission: the camera, used only as the live window behind the Real-sky stars — never stored, never sent. Home city is picked once in the grown-up corner — never GPS.</li>
 </ul>
-<p>Built on a pedagogy research brief about how five-year-olds actually form (and mis-form) ideas about space — so the simplifications are honest ones she'll never have to unlearn.</p> </div> </details> <div class="flex items-center justify-between pt-2 border-t border-ink/5 mt-auto"> <span class="text-xs text-ink/50">2026-06-12</span> <a href="/builds/SophiasSpace-1.4.2.apk" download class="px-4 py-2 rounded-lg bg-accent text-white text-sm font-semibold hover:bg-accent/90">

1: # Implementation Plan: Reconciled Code Audit Fixes for v1.4.3 (build 11)
2: 
3: This plan implements the six verified code/UX defects identified in the recent fresh-eyes audit.
4: 
5: ## User Review Required
6: 
7: > [!NOTE]
8: > All changes are minor UX and stability improvements and adhere to the strict privacy and pedagogical constraints of the app. The next release will be versioned as **v1.4.3 (build 11)**.
9: 
10: ## Open Questions
11: 
12: None.
13: 
14: ## Proposed Changes
15: 
16: ### 1. Robust TTS Initialization and Fallback
17: 
18: #### [MODIFY] [NarrationManager.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/NarrationManager.kt)
19: - Keep track of an `initFailed` boolean flag.
20: - If TTS fails to initialize (`status != TextToSpeech.SUCCESS`) or if `Locale.US` is not supported, set `initFailed = true` and show a Toast warning: *"Text-to-Speech failed to start. Please check your system US-English voice settings!"*.
21: - When `speakText` is called, check if `initFailed` is true. If so, attempt to re-initialize TTS, providing an automatic retry mechanism if the TTS engine becomes available later.
22: 
23: ---
24: 
25: ### 2. Navigation Back-Press Crash Guard
26: 
27: #### [MODIFY] [Navigation.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/Navigation.kt)
28: - Guard the system back-press event for `Home` to only pop the backstack if it contains more than just the home sc
<truncated 2003 bytes>
/HomeScreen.kt)
63: - Wrap the sticker text inside a single-line horizontally scrollable `Row` (`horizontalScroll(rememberScrollState())`) to prevent layout squishing when all 27 stickers are earned.
64: 
65: ---
66: 
67: ### 7. Release Configuration and Build Notes
68: 
69: #### [MODIFY] [build.gradle.kts](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/build.gradle.kts)
70: - Bump `versionCode` to `11`.
71: - Bump `versionName` to `"1.4.3"`.
72: 
73: #### [MODIFY] [sophiaspace-build-dashboard.html](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/docs/sophiaspace-build-dashboard.html)
74: - Append a dated changelog entry for **v1.4.3 (build 11)** in the `CHANGES` array.
75: 
76: #### [MODIFY] [index.html](file:///y:/ReynoldsFamily/builds/index.html)
77: - Update the builds website card version to `v1.4.3 · build 11` and the download path to `SophiasSpace-1.4.3.apk`.
78: 
79: ---
80: 
81: ## Verification Plan
82: 
83: ### Automated Tests
84: - Run `.\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest` to verify all 57 tests remain green.
85: 
86: ### Manual Verification
87: 1. **Back-press**: Navigate to Home and press the system back button. Verify that the app does not crash and handles back-press gracefully.
88: 2. **Drag-during-reset**: Move a planet, press "Put It Back", and simultaneously try to drag the planet mid-reset. Verify the planet ignores the drag and resets cleanly.
89: 3. **Sensor accuracy**: Verify that starting a new session in Magic Window does not immediately play the calibration audio cue unless accuracy actually degrades.
90: 4. **Sticker scroll**: Verify that when multiple stickers are present, the sticker shelf scrolls horizontally without compressing the navigation buttons on Home.
91: 5. **Ducking in-flight**: Trigger a long sound effect (e.g. restoration fanfare) and trigger a narration line immediately after. Verify that the active sound volume is ducked dynamically.
92:

1: # Walkthrough: Audit Findings and Release v1.4.2 (build 10)
2: 
3: This document details the audit fixes, verification results, and release deployment for version **v1.4.2 (build 10)** of Sophia's Space.
4: 
5: ## 🛠️ Changes Implemented
6: 
7: ### 1. Orientation Calibration Warning
8: - **Modified**: [OrientationProvider.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/sky/OrientationProvider.kt) + [SkyViewScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt) + [Scripts.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/Scripts.kt)
9: - **Fix**: Captured the calibration accuracy status in `OrientationProvider` via a StateFlow. When pointing in Magic Window or AR mode, if accuracy drops to low/unreliable, the app plays a debounced `SKY_CALIBRATE` prompt ("Hmm, the compass feels dizzy! Draw a big lazy eight...") at most once every 60 seconds.
10: 
11: ### 2. Versioned Synth Sound Cache
12: - **Modified**: [SoundSynth.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/SoundSynth.kt)
13: - **Fix**: Upgraded cache to `SYNTH_VERSION = 2`. Files are now saved as `synth_v2_<name>.wav`. Legacy cache files not matching the new prefix are automatically deleted from `cacheDir` at startup, ensuring any changes to the synthesizers are 
<truncated 2173 bytes>
1: ### 7. Documentation and Release Configuration
32: - **Modified**: [sophiaspace-build-dashboard.html](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/docs/sophiaspace-build-dashboard.html) + [build.gradle.kts](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/build.gradle.kts) + [index.html](file:///y:/ReynoldsFamily/builds/index.html)
33: - **Fix**: Ticked off Phase-0 validation tasks in the build dashboard. Bumped code version to `10` / name `1.4.2` in `build.gradle.kts` and appended build changelog notes. Updated the builds website card and download path to match the new version.
34: 
35: ---
36: 
37: ## 🧪 Verification & Verification Results

40: Added new unit tests in [SkyViewTest.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/test/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sky/SkyViewTest.kt) to verify the correctness of the `findNearestPlanet` algorithm.
41: - **Command**: `.\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest`
42: - **Result**: `BUILD SUCCESSFUL` with all **57/57** unit tests green.
43: 
44: ### 2. Manual Verification & On-Device Run
45: - **Build**: Successfully compiled `SophiasSpace-1.4.2` debug build.
46: - **ADB Port**: Active connection to wireless adb at `192.168.50.67:44297`.
47: - **Copy**: APK copied to `Y:\ReynoldsFamily\builds\SophiasSpace-1.4.2.apk`.
48: - **Deployment**: Successfully pushed and installed to the device via ADB:
49:   ```powershell
50:   adb -s 192.168.50.67:44297 install -r app-debug.apk
51:   ```
52: - **On-Device validation**:
53:   - The **twinkle optimization** shows visually identical smooth rendering but is frame-rate capped to 20 fps.
54:   - The **Moon** correctly acts as a dwell target in Sky View, pops, speaks, and awards the `🌖` sticker.

6:         conn = asyncio.open_connection(ip, port)
7:         reader, writer = await asyncio.wait_for(conn, timeout=0.15)
8:         writer.close()
9:         await writer.wait_closed()
10:         print(f"FOUND:{port}", flush=True)
11:         return port
12:     except Exception:
13:         return None
14: 
15: async def main():
16:     ip = "192.168.50.67"
17:     start_port = 35000
18:     end_port = 45000
19:     
20:     print(f"Scanning {ip} from {start_port} to {end_port}...")
21:     tasks = []
22:     # Scan in batches to avoid OS resource limits
23:     batch_size = 500
24:     for i in range(start_port, end_port, batch_size):
25:         batch_tasks = [try_connect(ip, port) for port in range(i, min(i + batch_size, end_port))]
26:         results = await asyncio.gather(*batch_tasks)
27:         found = [r for r in results if r is not None]
28:         if found:
29:             print(f"Scan complete. Active port: {found[0]}")
30:             sys.exit(0)
31:             
32:     print("No open ports found.")
33: 
34: if __name__ == "__main__":
35:     asyncio.run(main())
36:

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-12 23:52:49 UTC)

I will write a diagnostic port scanner script to `scratch/scan_diag.py` with smaller batches (200), a progress reporter, and a timeout of 1.5 seconds. This will prevent overloading the OS socket pool and allow us to monitor progress.


### 🛠️ Tool Executions

<details>
<summary><b>Call `write_to_file` - *"Writing diagnostic scanner script"*</b></summary>

Arguments:
```json
{
  "ArtifactMetadata": "{\"RequestFeedback\":false,\"Summary\":\"Diagnostic port scanner for wireless ADB.\",\"UserFacing\":false}",
  "CodeContent": "\"import asyncio\\nimport sys\\n\\nasync def try_connect(ip, port):\\n    try:\\n        conn = asyncio.open_connection(ip, port)\\n        reader, writer = await asyncio.wait_for(conn, timeout=1.5)\\n        writer.close()\\n        await writer.wait_closed()\\n        print(f\\\"FOUND:{port}\\\", flush=True)\\n        return port\\n    except Exception:\\n        return None\\n\\nasync def main():\\n    ip = \\\"192.168.50.67\\\"\\n    start_port = 30000\\n    end_port = 50000\\n    batch_size = 200\\n    \\n    print(f\\\"Scanning {ip} from {start_port} to {end_port}...\\\", flush=True)\\n    \\n    for i in range(start_port, end_port, batch_size):\\n        if i % 2000 == 0:\\n            print(f\\\"Progress: reached port {i}...\\\", flush=True)\\n        batch_tasks = [try_connect(ip, port) for port in range(i, min(i + batch_size, end_port))]\\n        results = await asyncio.gather(*batch_tasks)\\n        found = [r for r in results if r is not None]\\n        if found:\\n            print(f\\\"Scan complete. Active port: {found[0]}\\\", flush=True)\\n            sys.exit(0)\\n            \\n    print(\\\"No open ports found.\\\", flush=True)\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    asyncio.run(main())\\n\"",
  "Description": "\"Write diagnostic port scanner.\"",
  "Overwrite": "true",
  "TargetFile": "\"C:\\\\Users\\\\thoma\\\\.gemini\\\\antigravity\\\\brain\\\\519c5a72-c69e-4845-a55c-8f0d04101e15\\\\scratch\\\\scan_diag.py\"",
  "toolAction": "\"Writing diagnostic scanner script\"",
  "toolSummary": "\"Write scratch script\""
}
```
</details>

(and mis-form) ideas about space - so the simplifications are honest ones she'll never have to unlearn.</p> </div>

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-12 23:57:40 UTC)

I will write a python script `scratch/scan_multi.py` to scan the port range `30000-50000` on the four IP addresses that match the Google MAC address vendor prefix (`44-07-0b`), as the phone may have changed its IP.


### 🛠️ Tool Executions

<details>
<summary><b>Call `write_to_file` - *"Writing multi-IP scanner script"*</b></summary>

Arguments:
```json
{
  "ArtifactMetadata": "{\"RequestFeedback\":false,\"Summary\":\"Scanner script for Google MAC IPs.\",\"UserFacing\":false}",
  "CodeContent": "\"import asyncio\\nimport sys\\n\\nasync def try_connect(ip, port):\\n    try:\\n        conn = asyncio.open_connection(ip, port)\\n        reader, writer = await asyncio.wait_for(conn, timeout=1.0)\\n        writer.close()\\n        await writer.wait_closed()\\n        print(f\\\"FOUND:{ip}:{port}\\\", flush=True)\\n        return ip, port\\n    except Exception:\\n        return None\\n\\nasync def scan_ip(ip):\\n    start_port = 30000\\n    end_port = 50000\\n    batch_size = 200\\n    print(f\\\"Scanning {ip} from {start_port} to {end_port}...\\\", flush=True)\\n    for i in range(start_port, end_port, batch_size):\\n        batch_tasks = [try_connect(ip, port) for port in range(i, min(i + batch_size, end_port))]\\n        results = await asyncio.gather(*batch_tasks)\\n        found = [r for r in results if r is not None]\\n        if found:\\n            print(f\\\"Found active ADB port on {ip}: {found[0][1]}\\\", flush=True)\\n            return found[0]\\n    return None\\n\\nasync def main():\\n    ips = [\\\"192.168.50.72\\\", \\\"192.168.50.163\\\", \\\"192.168.50.193\\\", \\\"192.168.50.204\\\"]\\n    for ip in ips:\\n        res = await scan_ip(ip)\\n        if res:\\n            print(f\\\"SUCCESS: {res[0]}:{res[1]}\\\")\\n            sys.exit(0)\\n    print(\\\"No ADB port found on any of the target IPs.\\\")\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    asyncio.run(main())\\n\"",
  "Description": "\"Write multi-IP port scanner.\"",
  "Overwrite": "true",
  "TargetFile": "\"C:\\\\Users\\\\thoma\\\\.gemini\\\\antigravity\\\\brain\\\\519c5a72-c69e-4845-a55c-8f0d04101e15\\\\scratch\\\\scan_multi.py\"",
  "toolAction": "\"Writing multi-IP scanner script\"",
  "toolSummary": "\"Write scratch script\""
}
```
</details>

  "CodeContent": "\"# Walkthrough: Audit Findings and Release v1.4.3 (build 11)\\n\\nThis document details the audit fixes, verification results, and release deployment for version **v1.4.3 (build 11)** of Sophia's Space.\\n\\n## \ud83d\udee0\ufe0f Changes Implemented\\n\\n### 1. Robust TTS Initialization and Fallback\\n- **Modified**: [NarrationManager.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/audio/NarrationManager.kt)\\n- **Fix**: Added tracking of `initFailed`. If the TTS engine fails to initialize (or if the US English locale is unavailable), a Toast message is displayed: *\\\"Text-to-Speech failed to start. Please check your system US-English voice settings!\\\"*. Additionally, if `speakText` is subsequently called and the engine is in a failed state, it attempts to re-initialize TTS automatically, recovery-guarding subsequent narration requests.\\n\\n### 2. Navigation Back-Press Crash Guard\\n- **Modified**: [Navigation.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/Navigation.kt)\\n- **Fix**: Guarded the system back-press callback on the `Home` screen so that it only pops the backstack if it contains more than one screen (`backStack.size > 1`). This prevents popping the root home screen, which was causing Jetpack Navigation3 to crash when pressing back on the home dashboard.\\n\\n### 3. Drag-During-Reset Sandbox Guard\\n- **Modified**: [SandboxScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sandbox/SandboxScreen.kt)\\n- **Fix**: Added logic to ignore and abort planet drag gestures when the reset tween is active. Checked inside `onDrag` and the `release()` helper to prevent user touch input from overriding target coordinates during tween animation.\\n\\n### 4. 1: # Sophia's Space — Code-Derived Outline (no docs consulted)
2: 
3: > **PROMPT — paste this to your agent:**
4: >
5: > Below is an independent outline of what this Android app does, derived **exclusively from reading the source code** (`app/` tree, v1.4.2 / versionCode 10) — no design docs, dashboards, or prior prompts were consulted. Compare it, section by section, against (a) the prompts you have been given about this app and (b) your own current understanding of the codebase. Report: features described to you that the code does NOT implement; behavior the code implements that you were never told about; and places where your understanding of HOW something works differs from what's outlined here. Do not fix anything yet — produce the discrepancy report first.
6: 
7: ---
8: 
9: ## What the app is (as the code tells it)
10: 
11: **"Sophia's Space"** (`com.example.sophiaspace`) — a single-activity Jetpack Compose Android app (minSdk 24, targetSdk 36, portrait-locked, always-dark space theme, `allowBackup=false`). One permission: CAMERA, used solely as a live preview layer. Every interaction speaks through a single shared TextToSpeech engine with playful first-person-plural lines addressed to a child named Sophia ("Welcome to space, Sophia!") — the app is unmistakably built for **one specific pre-reader child**: text is decorative, meaning is carried by voice, touch targets are huge, and nothing has a fail state.
12: 
13: ### Navigation (Navigation3, 8 destinations)
14: Home grid → Playground (sandbox), Spinning (orbits), D
<truncated 7591 bytes>
io/SoundSynth.kt`)
50: 5. **The Moon dwell-find has no target feedback.** Planets get a growing gold ring during dwell and a celebration ring on find; the Moon target gets neither — only the center reticle fills. Inconsistent with how the rest of finding teaches itself. (`ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt`)
51: 6. **"Once per session" welcome is actually once per process.** `welcomedThisSession` is a top-level var: warm relaunches never re-welcome; a mid-session process death re-welcomes. (`ui/home/HomeScreen.kt`)
52: 7. **AR field-of-view math assumes Pixel-typical camera behavior** — default 4:3 full-active-array preview with FILL_CENTER cropping. Devices where CameraX selects a different stream aspect or a cropped sensor will get a mis-scaled overlay; there is no runtime cross-check. (`ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt`)
53: 8. **Stale sensor-accuracy can fire one spurious calibration prompt.** `OrientationProvider._accuracy` retains its last value across `stop()`; re-entering a sensor mode can re-trigger the lazy-8 line off old data before a fresh reading lands (debounce limits it to once). (`sky/OrientationProvider.kt`)
54: 9. **Sky catalog accuracy is untested by construction.** Tests verify referential integrity (every line endpoint exists), not that RA/Dec values match the real sky — coordinate errors would pass CI invisibly and only show up against the actual night sky.
55: 10. **Whole-screen recomposition at 60 fps is the rendering strategy.** Sandbox, lessons, and Sky View all write a `frameTick` state every frame and read it in composition; it works, but it's the app's largest performance liability and is load-bearing — "optimizing away" the reads breaks redrawing. Handle with care.
56: 11. **The sticker shelf has no overflow plan.** 27 earnable stickers (7 lesson + 12 constellations + 7 planets + moon) join one wrapping Text line that compresses the Home grid on small screens as the collection grows. (`ui/home/HomeScreen.kt`)
57:

1: ---
2: title: "Sophia's Space — Product Requirements"
3: project: SophiaSpace
4: version: 1.0-draft
5: date: 2026-06-09
6: author: Tom (with Claude)
7: status: Approved for build
8: ---
9: 
10: # Sophia's Space — A Solar System for a 5-Year-Old
11: 
12: ## Executive statement
13: 
14: Sophia's Space is a fully narrated, exploratory Android app that lets a 5-year-old pre-reader *feel* how the solar system works — not by reading about it, but by spinning the Earth with her finger, dragging the Moon out of its orbit and watching it fly away, launching rockets that miss Mars until she learns to aim where Mars is *going to be*, and holding the phone up to the real night sky to find the constellations hiding there. Everything speaks. Nothing punishes. Every broken thing can be put back.
15: 
16: The big ideas, at her level:
17: 
18: | Big idea | How she experiences it |
19: |---|---|
20: | Everything in space is spinning and circling | Planets loop the Sun on glowing trails; she can speed time up and watch a year happen |
21: | A day is the Earth spinning | She spins Earth with her finger and watches her little house go from sunshine to stars |
22: | Moon phases are sunlight + shadow | She drags the Moon around Earth and a "what we see from home" window shows the phase change live |
23: | Gravity holds it all together — and has consequences | A real (simplified) gravity simulation: move Earth closer to the Sun and it sweats; fling the Moon and it escapes |
24: | Rockets curve, and missions aim ahead | Straight up falls back down; sideways-fa
<truncated 7639 bytes>
 (designed-for, not built):** more constellations, seasonal-tilt lesson, parent-recorded voiceover, tablet layout, camera AR.
81: 
82: ## Risk register
83: 
84: | Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
85: |---|---|---|
86: | Rotation-vector sensor noisy/absent; magnetometer interference indoors | Medium | Phase 0 spike on real device; quaternion slerp smoothing; kid-friendly figure-8 calibration ("draw a lazy 8 in the air!"); drag mode is first-class, not a fallback |
87: | Azimuth needs declination + location | Certain | One-time city pick in grown-up settings; generous capture radii absorb ±10° |
88: | Physics blowup when she drops bodies onto each other | High — kids find it instantly | Softening, velocity clamp, world bounds, sub-step cap; JVM stress test (1000 random drags, assert no NaN/Inf) |
89: | TTS latency or robotic voice losing her | Medium | Warm-up at launch, buffered queue, short rhythmic lines; parent-recorded VO is the future option |
90: | Two sandbox scales (Sun vs Earth–Moon) confusing | Medium | Distinct backdrops, "let's zoom waaay in" framing narration; validate at GATE 1 |
91: | Drag-fling too imprecise for 5-yo motor skills | Medium | GATE 1 pivot: tap-body-then-arrow aiming (shares the rocket aim-arrow code) |
92: | Canvas star field performance | Low | ~450-star cap, batched drawPoints, dot-product culling, no per-frame allocation |
93: | Battery drain (sensor + 60 fps canvas) | Low | SENSOR_DELAY_GAME only on Sky View; unregister on pause; sim pauses when backgrounded |
94: | Scope creep across 6 modules | High | Gates are explicit stop points; Phases 3 and 4 independently shippable |
95: 
96: ## Decisions log
97: 
98: | Decision | Why | Status |
99: |---|---|---|
100: | Real mini-physics sandbox (not scripted what-ifs) | Honest cause-and-effect is the soul of the app; guards make it safe | Decided |

### 🛠️ Tool Executions

<details>
<summary><b>Call `write_to_file` - *"Creating hand-off prompt file"*</b></summary>

Arguments:
```json
{
  "ArtifactMetadata": "{\"RequestFeedback\":false,\"Summary\":\"Handoff prompt for merging Sophia's Space with preschool readiness and emotional learning features.\",\"UserFacing\":true}",
  "CodeContent": "\"# Handoff & Brainstorming Prompt: Sophia's Space + Preschool Readiness & Emotional Learning\\n\\nYou can copy and paste the text block below directly into another LLM conversation to instantly align that model with the exact code, architecture, pedagogy, and design guidelines of Sophia's Space, and prompt it to help you design the merged application.\\n\\n***\\n\\n```markdown\\n# Merge & Brainstorming Task: Sophia's Space (v1.4.3) + Preschool & Emotional Learning\\n\\nI am working on a research and design project to merge a custom Android application I built for my 5-year-old daughter, Sophia (called \\\"Sophia's Space\\\"), with emotional/social-emotional learning (SEL) and preschool-readiness concepts. \\n\\nBelow is the complete technical and pedagogical outline of \\\"Sophia's Space\\\" (as of version v1.4.3 / build 11), including its architecture, design rules, screen functionalities, and latest engine updates. \\n\\nRead this context carefully. Core Pedagogical & Design Rules (The Code's Philosophy)\\nThe codebase strictly enforces the following principles to ensure the app is a safe, encouraging, and educationally honest toy:\\n1. **Curiosity is Never Punished**: There are zero fail states, zero \\\"game over\\\" screens, and no resource limits (like fuel).
